text: Original Issue Date :	March 3, 2009 SOP Title :  	Attendance Policy Affected Departments :	PRC All

Policy and Purpose :	Human Resources Policy for Attendance - the purpose of this policy is to standardize the official rules, regulations, and processes for PRC’s Attendance Policy. It defines attendance, all relevant rules and requirements, and establishes the expectation for all Team Members to work their scheduled shifts in a punctual and consistent manner. The policy also outlines the criteria by which attendance will be measured and tracked. Leadership may adopt a more restrictive policy based on operational business needs with approval from the VP of HR/Talent Management / Division VP / General Manager. This policy has been revised and updated to include the following key changes: The maximum number of allowable occurrence points is increased to 15 The levels of notification are adjusted based on the 15-point provision For multi-day absences, each day counts as a separate occurrence unless a leave of absence is approved

The maximum number of allowable occurrence points is increased to 15 The levels of notification are adjusted based on the 15-point provision For multi-day absences, each day counts as a separate occurrence unless a leave of absence is approved A minimum of two (2) hours advance notice is required for call-offs Absences from mandatory meetings or trainings will count as occurrences Urgent Care and non-emergency doctor’s notes are not accepted to excuse attendance violations All occurrences will be tracked on a 365-day lookback period basis Each occurrence will remain on record for 365 days Early-outs will not be assigned points if they have been approved by management, regardless of who initiated the request Human Resources will be able to waive occurrences for unplanned absences with documentation of a bona fide emergency or severe extenuating circumstance ATTENDANCE STANDARDS Attendance Standards Overview

Human Resources will be able to waive occurrences for unplanned absences with documentation of a bona fide emergency or severe extenuating circumstance ATTENDANCE STANDARDS Attendance Standards Overview 1. 	To maintain the highest standards of guest service at PRC, we require Team Members to be punctual and present. 2. 	As a condition of employment, each Team Member agrees in writing to be available to work overtime, weekends, and holidays, or as otherwise directed by their department’s operational needs. 3. 	Minimizing absences and tardiness is an important part of overall job performance and will be considered in the performance review process. 4.	Exempt Team Members are expected to maintain exemplary attendance that meets or exceeds the minimum standards established for non-exempt Team Members by the occurrence point system. Attendance Requirements

3. 	Minimizing absences and tardiness is an important part of overall job performance and will be considered in the performance review process. 4.	Exempt Team Members are expected to maintain exemplary attendance that meets or exceeds the minimum standards established for non-exempt Team Members by the occurrence point system. Attendance Requirements Schedules are determined by departments, and Team Members are expected to know their schedules, make all preparations to be at work at their scheduled start time, be present at pre-shift meetings, and be ready to perform their job duties. Absent extenuating circumstances, a minimum two (2) hour notice is required for any call-off prior to a scheduled shift. Calls should be made to the Team Member’s direct supervisor or as otherwise directed by department protocol. 3.   	Team Members are to remain at work for their entire scheduled shift, except for break and meal periods.

Absent extenuating circumstances, a minimum two (2) hour notice is required for any call-off prior to a scheduled shift. Calls should be made to the Team Member’s direct supervisor or as otherwise directed by department protocol. 3.   	Team Members are to remain at work for their entire scheduled shift, except for break and meal periods. This requirement includes adherence with badge, uniform, dress code, and grooming policies. 4. 	Team Members must obtain prior approval from their Management for any change or deviation to their published schedule. 5. If Team Member requests time off, the Team Member must submit a “time off request” through PRC’s Global Human Resources (GHR) platform, in accordance with the “Paid Time Off (PTO)” standard operating procedure. Attendance Monitoring and Tracking Attendance is monitored by each department and through the use of the PRC’s timekeeping system, which tracks all timeclock punches in and out.

If Team Member requests time off, the Team Member must submit a “time off request” through PRC’s Global Human Resources (GHR) platform, in accordance with the “Paid Time Off (PTO)” standard operating procedure. Attendance Monitoring and Tracking Attendance is monitored by each department and through the use of the PRC’s timekeeping system, which tracks all timeclock punches in and out. Team Members are expected to complete their timeclock punches in accordance with PRC’s Timekeeping and Payroll provisions. 2. All requests for time off are to be approved and tracked by Management in PRC’s Global Human Resources (GHR) platform. 3. 	Management is responsible for monitoring attendance records to ensure accuracy and to make corrections as necessary. Use of PTO 1. 	Team Members must use available PTO for all personal absences, including vacation, sick days, holiday days, and personal time off.

3. 	Management is responsible for monitoring attendance records to ensure accuracy and to make corrections as necessary. Use of PTO 1. 	Team Members must use available PTO for all personal absences, including vacation, sick days, holiday days, and personal time off. 2. 	In the case of a planned absence, PTO must be requested via GHR and should be requested at least two (2) weeks in advance. Failure to provide at least two (2) weeks’ advance notice of a need for PTO may increase the likelihood that the PTO request will be denied based on scheduling or other operational reasons. 3. 	PTO requests for planned absences must be approved by Management before the Team Member takes the requested days off. 4. 	PTO requests submitted and approved in advance of planned absences are not subject to occurrence points. OCCURRENCE POINTS & PROCEDURES Occurrence Points

4. 	PTO requests submitted and approved in advance of planned absences are not subject to occurrence points. OCCURRENCE POINTS & PROCEDURES Occurrence Points The performance management process will be administered through a system of notifications in accordance with the level of occurrences outlined in the chart below. Performance management will apply in lieu of occurrences for exempt Team Members. Exempt Team Members are expected to maintain exemplary attendance that meets or exceeds the standards established by the occurrence schedule below. Occurrences are assigned on a PER INCIDENT BASIS for each of the occurrence types listed below. Multiple incidents in the same day can result in the assignment of multiple points. Occurrences are assigned per the following chart and on a 365-day lookback period basis. Fifteen or more occurrences in a 365-day period will result in separation. NUMBER OF OCCURRENCES ATTENDANCE VIOLATION

Fifteen or more occurrences in a 365-day period will result in separation. NUMBER OF OCCURRENCES ATTENDANCE VIOLATION Tardy 3 to 59 minutes with notification prior to the start of the Team Member’s shift Being unprepared at the start of the shift for any reason that prevents the Team Member from performing job duties, such as badge, grooming, uniform, etc., but is able to return to work within 59 minutes from start of shift with Management approval Taking a full or partial day of PTO requested through GHR, but NOT approved Call-outs with notification more than two hours before the start of the Team Member’s shift (including full or partial day absences) Tardy 3 to 59 minutes without notification prior to the start of the Team Member’s shift Tardy 1 hour or more with notification more than two hours before the start of the Team Member’s shift

Call-outs with notification more than two hours before the start of the Team Member’s shift (including full or partial day absences) Tardy 3 to 59 minutes without notification prior to the start of the Team Member’s shift Tardy 1 hour or more with notification more than two hours before the start of the Team Member’s shift Requesting and taking an early out (early-outs approved, directed, or requested by management are not subject to occurrence points) Being unprepared at the start of the shift for any reason that prevents the Team Member from performing job duties, such as badge, grooming, uniform, etc., where the Team Member is unable to return to work within 59 minutes from start of shift with Management approval Multi-day absences will be assigned separate occurrence points for each day Absence from a mandatory meeting or training No-call/No-show for a scheduled shift, full or partial day

Multi-day absences will be assigned separate occurrence points for each day Absence from a mandatory meeting or training No-call/No-show for a scheduled shift, full or partial day Tardy more than two (2) hours without notification, or with notification later than 2 hours after the start of the Team Member’s shift Unauthorized early-out on a blackout day or holiday Call-out/Absence on a scheduled black-out day or holiday, calculated per day (without documentation of a bona fide emergency or severe extenuating circumstance) Non-Occurrences Occurrences are not assigned for any of the following: Tardy or absence resulting from a shift change for which the Team Member was not given the required two-week prior notice, unless a shorter notice period is necessitated by business needs; Arrangements to have a qualified co-worker in the same job classification cover a Team Member’s shift without accrual of overtime, and approved by management;

Occurrences are not assigned for any of the following: Tardy or absence resulting from a shift change for which the Team Member was not given the required two-week prior notice, unless a shorter notice period is necessitated by business needs; Arrangements to have a qualified co-worker in the same job classification cover a Team Member’s shift without accrual of overtime, and approved by management; Verifiable instances of transportation delays approved by PRC HR Business Operations; Valid leaves of absence approved by PRC HR Benefits; Leaving work early upon management’s decision or request, including management’s affirmative response to a Team Member’s request to leave early; Leaving the workplace due to a workplace illness or injury at the direction of PRC; Unplanned absences waived by PRC HR Business Operations where there is documentation of a bona fide emergency or severe extenuating circumstance. a.

Leaving the workplace due to a workplace illness or injury at the direction of PRC; Unplanned absences waived by PRC HR Business Operations where there is documentation of a bona fide emergency or severe extenuating circumstance. a. In order to obtain a waiver, Team Members must present valid original documentation of a bona fide emergency or severe extenuating circumstance to the Team Member’s designated Human Resources Business Partner no later than the day the Team Member returns to work after the incident. Urgent care and non-emergency doctor’s notes, including telehealth services, are not accepted as valid documentation. b. 	Waivers may also be granted in cases where a Team Member must care for a spouse or domestic partner, dependent, or parent involved in a bona fide emergency or severe extenuating circumstance.

In order to obtain a waiver, Team Members must present valid original documentation of a bona fide emergency or severe extenuating circumstance to the Team Member’s designated Human Resources Business Partner no later than the day the Team Member returns to work after the incident. Urgent care and non-emergency doctor’s notes, including telehealth services, are not accepted as valid documentation. b. 	Waivers may also be granted in cases where a Team Member must care for a spouse or domestic partner, dependent, or parent involved in a bona fide emergency or severe extenuating circumstance. c. 	Occurrence received for missing a mandatory all-hands meeting may be granted a waiver if the Team Member watches the meeting in its entirety within thirty (30) days while on property during their regular work hours. Occurrences

b. 	Waivers may also be granted in cases where a Team Member must care for a spouse or domestic partner, dependent, or parent involved in a bona fide emergency or severe extenuating circumstance. c. 	Occurrence received for missing a mandatory all-hands meeting may be granted a waiver if the Team Member watches the meeting in its entirety within thirty (30) days while on property during their regular work hours. Occurrences Occurrences will be tracked on a 365-day lookback period basis, measured backward from the date of the Team Member’s most recent incident leading to an occurrence, and notifications will be given as follows: Number of Points Corresponding Notifications 2. 	Approved leaves of absence will cause the calculation of the active duration period to be placed “on hold” until the Team Member returns from the leave, per the “Time Away from Work Policy.”

Number of Points Corresponding Notifications 2. 	Approved leaves of absence will cause the calculation of the active duration period to be placed “on hold” until the Team Member returns from the leave, per the “Time Away from Work Policy.” This provision does not apply to approved intermittent leave of absence. DEFINITIONS & ADMINISTRATION Black-Out Days 1. 	Mandatory workdays, also referred to as “blackout days,” are days in the fiscal year during which all Team Members are expected to be available to work. 2. 	Departments may identify up to twelve (12) “black-out days” at the beginning of the fiscal year. Any changes or additions to black-out dates based upon individual division needs must be approved by the General Manager, and reasonable advance notice must be given to affected Team Members. Early-Outs 1.	An early-out occurs when Team Member leaves work with permission before the end of their scheduled shift.

Any changes or additions to black-out dates based upon individual division needs must be approved by the General Manager, and reasonable advance notice must be given to affected Team Members. Early-Outs 1.	An early-out occurs when Team Member leaves work with permission before the end of their scheduled shift. Leaving work early without permission constitutes an unapproved absence, and may also constitute job abandonment, depending on the circumstances. a.	If the early-out is approved by management, no occurrence will be assigned, and the Team Member will not be required to use available PTO hours. 2. 	Team Members will be selected for early-out opportunities, with consideration given to proper distribution of the opportunities to as many Team Members as possible over the course of time. Unapproved Absence An unapproved absence is any time away from scheduled hours without management approval (excluding LOAs or pre-approved PTO).

2. 	Team Members will be selected for early-out opportunities, with consideration given to proper distribution of the opportunities to as many Team Members as possible over the course of time. Unapproved Absence An unapproved absence is any time away from scheduled hours without management approval (excluding LOAs or pre-approved PTO). This may include taking unapproved PTO time, lack of call-in or notice for absence, unauthorized shift changes or switching of shifts, and leaving before the end of the scheduled shift without approval. No-Call/No-Show A “no-call/no-show” will occur if a Team Member fails to notify his/her supervisor within two (2) hours after the start of their scheduled shift, except where there is documentation of a bona fide emergency or severe extenuating circumstance. Any no-call/no-show will be deemed job abandonment after two (2) consecutive scheduled workdays.

No-Call/No-Show A “no-call/no-show” will occur if a Team Member fails to notify his/her supervisor within two (2) hours after the start of their scheduled shift, except where there is documentation of a bona fide emergency or severe extenuating circumstance. Any no-call/no-show will be deemed job abandonment after two (2) consecutive scheduled workdays. Two no-call/no-shows within a twelve-month period may result in separation, regardless of the Team Member’s number of occurrences. Documentation for Multi-Day Absences If a Team Member calls out sick for more than three (3) consecutive workdays, they are required to submit documentation from a health care provider certifying the medical necessity for the absence and providing the expected date of returning to work. Occurrences will still be incurred, unless a leave of absence is approved. Patterns Attendance patterns may result in performance management, in addition to the accrual of occurrence points.

Occurrences will still be incurred, unless a leave of absence is approved. Patterns Attendance patterns may result in performance management, in addition to the accrual of occurrence points. For purposes of this section, patterns include, but are not limited to, calling out before and/or after scheduled days off or PTO, weekends, black-out days, and/or holidays; receipt of three (3) “3rd and final” warnings due to attendance; and other absences and tardies with timing or repetition that would appear suspicious to a reasonable person.
Original Issue Date 3/30/2009 SOP Title Solicitation and Distribution Affected Departments : PRC ALL

Policy and Purpose: To provide a written procedure for all Team Members to follow regarding any Solicitation and Distribution at PR&C. This policy applies to solicitation and distribution activities conducted on the grounds or within buildings under the control of PR&C, including solicitation using PRC’s electronic mail network ("e-mail"). Procedure(s): In order to prevent interference with the operations of PR&C and to maintain our responsibilities to our guests and customers, the following rules have been adopted with regard to solicitation and the distribution of materials: PRC EMPLOYEES Employees of PR&C may not solicit during working time for any purpose. Working time is defined below. Employees may not distribute literature during working time for any purpose. Employees may not distribute literature at any time for any purpose in working areas NON PR&C EMPLOYEES

Employees may not distribute literature during working time for any purpose. Employees may not distribute literature at any time for any purpose in working areas NON PR&C EMPLOYEES Persons who are not employed by PR&C may not solicit or distribute literature on PR&C property at any time for any purpose without the express written permission of the Tribe. WORKING TIME. “Working time” includes the working time of both the employee doing the soliciting and distributing and the employee to whom the soliciting or distributing is being directed. Working time does not include break periods, meal periods or any other specified periods during the work day when employees are properly not engaged in performing their duties.” PR&C provides Announcement Boards where staff members may post items approved by HR/Talent Management.

“Working time” includes the working time of both the employee doing the soliciting and distributing and the employee to whom the soliciting or distributing is being directed. Working time does not include break periods, meal periods or any other specified periods during the work day when employees are properly not engaged in performing their duties.” PR&C provides Announcement Boards where staff members may post items approved by HR/Talent Management. No staff member or non-staff member is allowed to post any notice on PR&C grounds or premises without specific prior written permission from HR/Talent Management.

SCOPE All Pechanga Resort & Casino Team Members PURPOSE This  Code  of  Business  Conduct  and  Ethics  outlines  expected  behaviors  for  all  Pechanga  Team  Members  and  Management, independent  contractors,  consultants,  agents,  officers  and  directors,  and  officials  of  Pechanga  Resort  &  Casino  and  Pechanga Development Corporation.

All Pechanga Resort & Casino Team Members PURPOSE This  Code  of  Business  Conduct  and  Ethics  outlines  expected  behaviors  for  all  Pechanga  Team  Members  and  Management, independent  contractors,  consultants,  agents,  officers  and  directors,  and  officials  of  Pechanga  Resort  &  Casino  and  Pechanga Development Corporation. In this Code, we refer to all of the foregoing individuals as ''Team Members''. When this Code refers to ''we'', ''us'', or ''our'', it is referring to Pechanga Resort & Casino and Pechanga Development Corporation. PR&C will conduct its business fairly, impartially, in an ethical and proper manner, and in full compliance with all applicable laws and regulations. In  conducting  its  business,  integrity  must  underlie  all  PR&C  relationships,  including  those  with  guests,  suppliers, communities  and  among  Team  Members. The  highest  standards  of  ethical  business  conduct  are  required  of  all  PR&C  Team Members/Management in the performance of their responsibilities.

PR&C will conduct its business fairly, impartially, in an ethical and proper manner, and in full compliance with all applicable laws and regulations. In  conducting  its  business,  integrity  must  underlie  all  PR&C  relationships,  including  those  with  guests,  suppliers, communities  and  among  Team  Members. The  highest  standards  of  ethical  business  conduct  are  required  of  all  PR&C  Team Members/Management in the performance of their responsibilities. Team Members and Management will not engage in conduct, or in any activity that may raise questions as to PR&C's honesty, impartiality or reputation, or other conduct that creates a negative public image of PR&C. Misconduct and the appearance of impropriety should be avoided at all times. Team Members/Management will ensure the following: § Do not engage in any activity that might create a conflict of interest for PR&C or for themselves individually.

Misconduct and the appearance of impropriety should be avoided at all times. Team Members/Management will ensure the following: § Do not engage in any activity that might create a conflict of interest for PR&C or for themselves individually. § Do not take advantage of their PR&C position to seek personal gain through the inappropriate use of non-public PR&C information; they do not abuse their position to seek personal gain. §  Will  follow  all  restrictions  on  use  and  disclosure  of  information. This  includes  following  all  requirements  for  protecting  PR&C information and ensuring that non-Pechanga proprietary information is used and disclosed only as authorized by the owner of the information or otherwise permitted by law. § Observe that fair dealing is the foundation for all of our transactions and interactions. § Will protect all property, guest and supplier assets and use them only for appropriate PR&C approved activities.

This  includes  following  all  requirements  for  protecting  PR&C information and ensuring that non-Pechanga proprietary information is used and disclosed only as authorized by the owner of the information or otherwise permitted by law. § Observe that fair dealing is the foundation for all of our transactions and interactions. § Will protect all property, guest and supplier assets and use them only for appropriate PR&C approved activities. § Without exception, they will comply with all applicable laws, rules and regulations. § Will immediately report any illegal or unethical conduct to Management or other appropriate authorities (i.e. Dept. of Public Safety, Pechanga Gaming Commission, and Department of Ethics). Every Team Member has the responsibility to ask questions, seek guidance and report suspected violations of this Code of Conduct to their immediate Supervisor/Manager or the confidential Ethics Hotline (1-888-396-9040) or Website at www.ethicspoint.com.

Ethical Professional Standards All  Team  Members  must  sign  and  abide  by  the  Pechanga  Resort  &  Casino  Code  of  Business  Conduct  and  Ethics. This  document requires that Team Members understand the code, ask questions, seek guidance, report suspected violations, and express concerns regarding compliance with this policy and related procedures. The Pechanga Development Corporation Department of Ethics in conjunction with the PR&C Human Resources Dept. will administer ethics and professional standards programs to promote its commitment to integrity and values as set forth by the Code of Business Conduct and Ethics. The Code also ensures that all PR&C Team Members and Leaders maintain compliance with all ethics related laws,  rules,  and  regulations.

This  document requires that Team Members understand the code, ask questions, seek guidance, report suspected violations, and express concerns regarding compliance with this policy and related procedures. The Pechanga Development Corporation Department of Ethics in conjunction with the PR&C Human Resources Dept. will administer ethics and professional standards programs to promote its commitment to integrity and values as set forth by the Code of Business Conduct and Ethics. The Code also ensures that all PR&C Team Members and Leaders maintain compliance with all ethics related laws,  rules,  and  regulations. These  programs  will  inform  Team  Members  of  PR&C  policies  and  procedures  regarding  ethical professional standards and help them to resolve questions and to report suspected violations. Supervisors/Managers are responsible for supporting the implementation of ethics and professional standards programs, and monitoring compliance with PR&C's values and ethical  professional  standards  guidelines  through  such  programs.

These  programs  will  inform  Team  Members  of  PR&C  policies  and  procedures  regarding  ethical professional standards and help them to resolve questions and to report suspected violations. Supervisors/Managers are responsible for supporting the implementation of ethics and professional standards programs, and monitoring compliance with PR&C's values and ethical  professional  standards  guidelines  through  such  programs. Supervisors/Managers  are  responsible  for  creating  an  open  and honest environment in which Team Members feel comfortable bringing issues forward. Retaliation against Team Members who raise genuine concerns will not be tolerated. Conflicts of Interest All of us must be able to perform our duties and exercise judgment without outside influence or impairment, or the appearance of outside influence or impairment, due to any activity, interest or relationship that arises outside of work.

Retaliation against Team Members who raise genuine concerns will not be tolerated. Conflicts of Interest All of us must be able to perform our duties and exercise judgment without outside influence or impairment, or the appearance of outside influence or impairment, due to any activity, interest or relationship that arises outside of work. Put more simply, when our loyalty to advance the best interests of Pechanga are affected by actual or potential benefit or influence from an outside source, a conflict of interest exists. We  should  all  be  aware  of  any  potential  influences  that  impact  or  appear  to impact our loyalty to Pechanga. In general, you should avoid situations where your personal interests conflict, or appear to conflict, with those of Pechanga. It is not possible to describe every conflict of interest, but some situations that could cause a conflict of interest include:

We  should  all  be  aware  of  any  potential  influences  that  impact  or  appear  to impact our loyalty to Pechanga. In general, you should avoid situations where your personal interests conflict, or appear to conflict, with those of Pechanga. It is not possible to describe every conflict of interest, but some situations that could cause a conflict of interest include: Doing  business  with  family  members,  a  member  of  the  Tribal  Council,  a  member  of  the  Board  of  Directors  of  the Pechanga Development Corporation or a member of the Pechanga Gaming Commission; Having a financial interest in another company with whom we do business; Taking a second job; Managing your own business; Serving as a director or officer of another business; Serving as a director or officer of a non-profit or charitable organization; and Diverting a business opportunity from a Pechanga business enterprise to another company.

Serving as a director or officer of another business; Serving as a director or officer of a non-profit or charitable organization; and Diverting a business opportunity from a Pechanga business enterprise to another company. Any time you believe a conflict of interest may exist, you MUST disclose the potential conflict of interest to your supervisor and to the Director of Ethics. If you are not comfortable reporting a conflict to your supervisor, consult with one of the designated members of management described in the section of this code entitled ''Asking for Help and Reporting Concerns.'' You can also call the Ethics HelpLine at 1-888396-9040, make a report at the Pechanga Ethics Website: www.ethicspoint.com, or send an email to ethics@pechanga.com.

With respect to a potential conflict reported by a senior manager or executive officer of the Pechanga Resort & Casino or a member of the Board of Directors of the Pechanga Development Corporation (sometimes referred to in this Code as the ''Board''), the Board will evaluate the conflict and make all determinations. The Board of Directors will review the potential conflict and determine if a conflict in fact exists. If a conflict does exist, the Board of Directors will then determine if the conflict would have an adverse impact on the interests of Pechanga. If a conflict does exist, the Board of Directors will then determine if the conflict would have an adverse impact on the interests of Pechanga. In certain cases, an activity or transaction may be approved despite the existence of a conflict if no adverse impact exists. For example, we may approve of the following circumstances: a director, officer or Team Member serving on a charitable board provided that such individual does not make funding decisions with respect to the charitable organization a  business  transaction  with  a  company  in  which  a  director ,  officer  or  Team  Member  has  a  material  financial  interest  if  that transaction is on fair market terms which are no different than we would receive from a completely independent vendor. Sometimes our Team Members desire to take additional part-time jobs or do other work after hours, such as consulting or other feeearning services. Violence in the Workplace The safety and security of PR&C Team Members and guests is of vital importance. PR&C has a Zero Tolerance policy for workplace violence. Acts  or  threats  of  physical  violence,  including  intimidation,  harassment  and/or  coercion,  which  involve  or  affect  PR&C  or which occur on PR&C property will not be tolerated.

The safety and security of PR&C Team Members and guests is of vital importance. PR&C has a Zero Tolerance policy for workplace violence. Acts  or  threats  of  physical  violence,  including  intimidation,  harassment  and/or  coercion,  which  involve  or  affect  PR&C  or which occur on PR&C property will not be tolerated. Workplace violence is conduct which is intended to cause another individual to reasonably fear for his or her personal safety or the safety of his or her family, friends, and/or property. It also includes conduct that is intended to create an abusive or intimidating work environment for one or more Team Members. If the matter is urgent and an immediate threat to life, property or the safety of others exists, a Team Member shall immediately notify the PR&C Department of Public Safety by dialing 770-2610. When the risk has passed or been neutralized, make an immediate report to the Supervisor.

It also includes conduct that is intended to create an abusive or intimidating work environment for one or more Team Members. Original Issue Date :		May 10, 2021 SOP Title :		PRC Lactation Policy Affected Departments :		PRC- ALL

Policy and Purpose :		The purpose of this policy is to standardize the process for allowing team members time to use the lactation room. PRC recognizes the nutritional and health benefit to children from receiving mother’s milk, and desires to provide a room that makes this benefit available to nursing employees. A. Overview : PRC will provide a reasonable amount of break time and a secure environment to any employee desiring to express breast milk for her infant child. PRC has made private lactation rooms available for this purpose. Lactation breaks should be taken concurrently with other scheduled rest breaks to the extent possible. Any break time already provided to the employee in such circumstances, will be paid. However, if such break time does not run concurrently with the employee’s normal break times, such time may be unpaid.

Lactation breaks should be taken concurrently with other scheduled rest breaks to the extent possible. Any break time already provided to the employee in such circumstances, will be paid. However, if such break time does not run concurrently with the employee’s normal break times, such time may be unpaid. Nonexempt employees must clock out for any lactation breaks that do not run concurrently with normally scheduled rest breaks. Any such breaks will be unpaid. Employees needing an accommodation should submit a request for to the benefits department. PRC reserves the right to deny an employee’s request if the additional break time will seriously disrupt operations. B. Lactation Room Participation : In order to be able to utilize the lactation room, team members need to contact the Occupational Health Office at (951) 770-2696, on the first (1 st ) of each month in order to maintain access.

In order to be able to utilize the lactation room, team members need to contact the Occupational Health Office at (951) 770-2696, on the first (1 st ) of each month in order to maintain access. Team member’s use of the lactation room will take place during their personal breaks, on their own time, or before or after work. Team member’s use of the lactation room is NOT associated with their job function, and this activity does not fall within the course and/or scope of any team member’s employment. The lactation room should be left in a clean and orderly manner. However, should the lactation room need attention, EVS should be contacted. Team members who utilize the lactation room agree that they will conduct themselves in accordance with the PRC Performance Management & Standards of Conduct Policy.

Team members are not permitted to brings guests into the lactation room or use their badge/card to allow others admittance into the room. In addition, team members are not allowed to use their badge access to open the lactation room door for another team member, if their access has been disabled. Team members who choose to use the lactation room agree that the room is to be used only for lactation purposes. The lactation room is not to be used for regular breaks, sleeping, or other activities that are inconsistent with lactation.

In addition, team members are not allowed to use their badge access to open the lactation room door for another team member, if their access has been disabled. The lactation room is not to be used for regular breaks, sleeping, or other activities that are inconsistent with lactation. Team members may eat lunch while in the lactation room, however, no food may be stored in the room and team members must remove all related trash each time they eat in the room. The microwave in the lactation room is to be used for sanitizing purposes only, and is not to be used for warming up food or drinks. Spraying of any kind of perfume, aromatic sprays or scents is not permitted in the lactation room.

PURPOSE To establish a consistent method for the maintenance and management of official and departmental personnel records. SCOPE Human Resource/Talent Management

To establish a consistent method for the maintenance and management of official and departmental personnel records. SCOPE Human Resource/Talent Management All PR&C Team Members PRACTICE: Pechanga Resort & Casino (PR&C) team members have a right to inspect certain documents in their personnel file in the presence of a PR&C representative at a mutually convenient time. No copies of any documents in the file may be made, with the exception of documents that the team member themselves have previously signed. If there is a request for a copy of a document, the team member must request this in writing, by date, signature and current badge number. The team member may add comments to any disputed item in the file, by signing with the date, team member's signature and current badge number. PR& C will restrict disclosure to the team member's personnel file to only authorized individuals within PR&C.

If there is a request for a copy of a document, the team member must request this in writing, by date, signature and current badge number. The team member may add comments to any disputed item in the file, by signing with the date, team member's signature and current badge number. PR& C will restrict disclosure to the team member's personnel file to only authorized individuals within PR&C. Any request for information contained in personnel files must be directed to the Human Resources/Talent Management Department. Only the Human Resource/Talent Management Department is authorized to release information about current or former team members. Disclosure of personnel information to outside sources will be limited. However, PR&C will cooperate with requests from authorized law enforcement or local, state or federal agencies conducting official investigations and as otherwise legally required.

Only the Human Resource/Talent Management Department is authorized to release information about current or former team members. Disclosure of personnel information to outside sources will be limited. However, PR&C will cooperate with requests from authorized law enforcement or local, state or federal agencies conducting official investigations and as otherwise legally required. Upon request, former team members will be provided with copies of documents in their file that they have previously signed. Any deviation from this policy must be approved in writing by the Vice President of Human Resources/Talent Management. Original Policy Date: 5/10/2008 Version Number: 3
Original Issue Date :	February 28, 2013 SOP Title :	TEAM MEMBER BADGE/LICENSE RETRIEVAL PROCEDURE Affected Departments : PRC MANAGEMENT/HUMAN RESOURCES/BENEFITS

Policy and Purpose :	Standard Operating Procedures for Team Member Badge/License Retrieval Procedure. The purpose of this policy is to standardize the process of Team Member Badge/License Retrieval Procedure. It is intended to provide insight, order, and accountability for all employees subject to such policies in Team Member Badge/License Retrieval Procedure. Updated policy since Benefits moved from the basement to HR second floor, merged the process since now there is only one front desk TM. SEPARATION/SUSPENSION - PRC Management/Human Resources : Involuntary Separation : Compliance Officer/DPS: A Compliance Officer/DPS TM is to be contacted by the designated Operations Partner to be present during all TM separation meetings and is to obtain TM badge during the separation meeting and return to the Gaming Commission same day. Designated HR Operations Partner to ensure applicable Employee Action Notice (EAN) is processed same day. Suspension : In-person Suspension Manager:

: In-person Suspension Manager: Should a TM be suspended in-person, the Manager is to obtain the TM badge and immediately notify the designated HR Operations Partner of the suspension and forward badge to the designated HR Operations Partner. If the designated HR Operations Partner is not available, the Manager is to immediately notify the HR Director’s Administrative Assistant of the suspension. Manager to complete and process applicable Employee Action Notice (EAN) same day. HR Operations Partner: Should a TM be suspended in-person, a Compliance Officer/DPS is to be contacted by the designated Operations Partner to be present during the in-person separation meeting and is to obtain badge from TM and forward to the Gaming Commission same day. text: Original Issue Date :	March 20, 2019 SOP Title :	Benefits: Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy Affected Departments : 	All Departments
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

text: Policy and Purpose :	To provide eligible employees with protected leave in certain qualifying circumstances. (Please type your change comments or explanations of the new policy below) This policy repeals and replaces the following policies. Family Medical Leave of Absence Policy (issued 3/1/2013), #10788 Family Medical Leave Intermittent Call-in, #14003 Eligibility Requirements Team Members are eligible for leave under this policy if they: Have been employed by PRC for at least 12 months (the 12 months need not be consecutive), AND Have at least 1,250 hours of service for PRC in the “rolling” 12-month period measured backward from the date of leave. Qualifying Reasons for Leave PRC will provide up to 12 weeks of unpaid, job protected leave to eligible Team Members for the following reasons: A serious health condition
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

text: PRC will provide up to 12 weeks of unpaid, job protected leave to eligible Team Members for the following reasons: A serious health condition that makes the Team Member unable to perform his or her essential job functions, including but not limited to incapacity due to pregnancy, prenatal medical care or childbirth, and workers’ compensation injuries Workers compensation shall run concurrently with PML (see PRC’s Workers’ Compensation Policy) To care for the Team Member’s spouse, child, or parent, who has a serious health condition To care for the Team Member’s child after birth or placement with the Team Member for adoption or foster care Leave taken for the birth, adoption, or foster care placement of a Team Member’s child must be concluded within one year following the child’s birth or placement Spouses who are both employed by PRC are entitled to a combined 12 weeks of bonding leave per year
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

text: with the Team Member for adoption or foster care Leave taken for the birth, adoption, or foster care placement of a Team Member’s child must be concluded within one year following the child’s birth or placement Spouses who are both employed by PRC are entitled to a combined 12 weeks of bonding leave per year A qualifying exigency arising out of the fact that the Team Member’s spouse, child, or parent is a covered military member on covered active duty Qualifying exigencies may include attending certain military events, arranging for alternative childcare, addressing certain financial and legal arrangements, attending certain counseling sessions, and attending post-deployment reintegration briefings PRC will provide up to 26 weeks of unpaid, job protected leave to eligible Team Members to care for the Team Member’s spouse, child, parent, or next of kin who is a seriously injured or ill covered service member .
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

text: PRC will provide up to 26 weeks of unpaid, job protected leave to eligible Team Members to care for the Team Member’s spouse, child, parent, or next of kin who is a seriously injured or ill covered service member . This type of leave is counted cumulatively with any other leave taken under this policy, for a combined maximum of 26 weeks in a 12-month period
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

text: For purposes of this policy, a “covered service member” is: (1) a current member or veteran of the Armed Forces, including a member of the National Guard or Reserves, (2) who is undergoing medical treatment, recuperation, or therapy or, in the case of a veteran, who was a current member of the Armed Forces, National Guard or Reserves who was discharged or released under conditions other than dishonorable at any time within five years prior to the treatment; is otherwise in outpatient status; or is on the temporary disability retired list, (3) who has a serious injury or illness that may render the current member medically unfit to perform the duties of the member’s office, grade, rank or rating Definition of a serious health condition . For purposes of this policy, “serious health condition” means an illness, injury, impairment, or physical or mental condition that involves at least one of the following:
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

text: Definition of a serious health condition . For purposes of this policy, “serious health condition” means an illness, injury, impairment, or physical or mental condition that involves at least one of the following: Any period of incapacity or treatment connected with inpatient care ( i.e ., an overnight stay in a hospital, hospice, or residential medical care facility) Continuing treatment by a health care provider involving: A period of incapacity of more than three (3) consecutive calendar days; At least two treatments by a health care provider within 30 days after the first day of incapacity (unless extenuating circumstances prevent the second treatment); or At least one treatment by a health care provider within seven (7) days after the first day of incapacity that results in a regimen of continuing treatment under that provider’s care Any period of incapacity or treatment for incapacity due to pregnancy or prenatal care
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

text: At least two treatments by a health care provider within 30 days after the first day of incapacity (unless extenuating circumstances prevent the second treatment); or At least one treatment by a health care provider within seven (7) days after the first day of incapacity that results in a regimen of continuing treatment under that provider’s care Any period of incapacity or treatment for incapacity due to pregnancy or prenatal care Any period of incapacity or treatment for incapacity due to a chronic serious health condition that: Requires periodic visits (at least twice a year) for treatment by a health care provider; Continues over an extended time (including recurring episodes); and May cause episodic rather than a continuing period of incapacity ( e.g ., asthma, diabetes, epilepsy) Any period of incapacity that is permanent or long-term due to a condition for which treatment may not be effective ( e.g
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

text: ., asthma, diabetes, epilepsy) Any period of incapacity that is permanent or long-term due to a condition for which treatment may not be effective ( e.g ., Alzheimer's, stroke, terminal diseases, etc.); or Absences to receive multiple treatments (including recovery time) by or on referral by a health care provider for restorative surgery after an accident or other injury, or a condition that would probably result in incapacity for more than three (3) consecutive days without medical treatment ( e.g ., such as cancer (chemotherapy, radiation, etc.), severe arthritis (physical therapy), kidney disease (dialysis)). The following conditions ordinarily do not constitute “serious health conditions” under this policy: common cold, flu, ear aches, upset stomach, minor ulcers, headaches other than migraine, routine dental or orthodontia problems, periodontal disease, etc., unless complications arise; and conditions for which cosmetic treatments are administered ( e.g
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

text: not constitute “serious health conditions” under this policy: common cold, flu, ear aches, upset stomach, minor ulcers, headaches other than migraine, routine dental or orthodontia problems, periodontal disease, etc., unless complications arise; and conditions for which cosmetic treatments are administered ( e.g ., most treatments for acne or plastic surgery) unless inpatient hospital care is required or complications develop. Intermittent and Reduced Schedule Leave PML may be taken on an intermittent or reduced schedule basis when medically necessary or for the birth, adoption, or foster care placement of a child. Required certification must be provided to PRC prior to the commencement of intermittent or reduced schedule PML. Scheduling Planned Treatments, Intermittent Leave, and Reduced Schedule Leave
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

text: or for the birth, adoption, or foster care placement of a child. Required certification must be provided to PRC prior to the commencement of intermittent or reduced schedule PML. Scheduling Planned Treatments, Intermittent Leave, and Reduced Schedule Leave Team Members taking PML for planned medical treatment and/or on an intermittent or reduced schedule basis must consult with their supervisor and make a reasonable effort to schedule the PML so as not to unduly disrupt the Department’s operations, subject to the approval of the health care provider. If the Team Member fails to do so, PRC may initiate discussions with the Team Member and require the Team Member to attempt to make such arrangements, subject to the approval of the health care provider. This policy does not permit unscheduled and unpredictable, but cumulatively substantial, absences.
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

text: Team Members taking PML for planned medical treatment and/or on an intermittent or reduced schedule basis must consult with their supervisor and make a reasonable effort to schedule the PML so as not to unduly disrupt the Department’s operations, subject to the approval of the health care provider. If the Team Member fails to do so, PRC may initiate discussions with the Team Member and require the Team Member to attempt to make such arrangements, subject to the approval of the health care provider. This policy does not permit unscheduled and unpredictable, but cumulatively substantial, absences. Reliable attendance is considered a bona fide essential job requirement for all PRC Team Members, and this policy does not entitle Team Members to take unscheduled leave at a moment's notice. PML Request Procedures Team Member Notice Requirements.
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

text: Reliable attendance is considered a bona fide essential job requirement for all PRC Team Members, and this policy does not entitle Team Members to take unscheduled leave at a moment's notice. PML Request Procedures Team Member Notice Requirements. Where the need for PML is foreseeable, Team Members must provide 30 days’ advance notice of the need for leave, the reason, and the dates. When 30 days’ notice is not possible, or the need for leave is not foreseeable, the Team Member must provide notice as soon as practicable under the circumstances (generally within one or two business days after the Team Member learns of the need for the leave). In all but the most extraordinary circumstances, Team Members must notify PRC of the need for PML prior to the start of their shift. Failure to provide the required notice may result in denial or delay of the leave.
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

text: When 30 days’ notice is not possible, or the need for leave is not foreseeable, the Team Member must provide notice as soon as practicable under the circumstances (generally within one or two business days after the Team Member learns of the need for the leave). In all but the most extraordinary circumstances, Team Members must notify PRC of the need for PML prior to the start of their shift. Failure to provide the required notice may result in denial or delay of the leave. Compliance with PRC’s Usual and Customary Notice and Procedural Requirements. In addition to the PML notice requirements described in Section E.1 above, Team Members requesting PML under this policy must also comply with PRC’s usual and customary notice and procedural requirements for requesting leave, unless unusual circumstances justify the noncompliance.
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

text: In addition to the PML notice requirements described in Section E.1 above, Team Members requesting PML under this policy must also comply with PRC’s usual and customary notice and procedural requirements for requesting leave, unless unusual circumstances justify the noncompliance. Failure to do so may result in the delay or denial of PML, or performance management action for violation of PRC’s leave procedures. Notice and Procedural Requirements for Unscheduled Intermittent Leave. Team Members approved to use PML on an intermittent basis who must be absent from work because of an unforeseen event covered by their approved intermittent PML must comply with the following call-in procedures: If they must leave work early for a reason covered by their approved intermittent PML, notify their supervisor in person
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

text: Notice and Procedural Requirements for Unscheduled Intermittent Leave. Team Members approved to use PML on an intermittent basis who must be absent from work because of an unforeseen event covered by their approved intermittent PML must comply with the following call-in procedures: If they must leave work early for a reason covered by their approved intermittent PML, notify their supervisor in person Report their absence or early out to the Benefits “intermittent call off line” at (951) 770-8156, either within two (2) hours from the start of their shift or no later than two (2) hours after clocking out and provide the following information: Badge Number Department Date of absence Number of hours used toward intermittent leave Amount of PTO available

source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

text: The leave will be placed on a pending status until all required medical certification is received and any needed clarification has been obtained. Once PRC has sufficient information to determine whether the Team Member is eligible for protected leave under this policy ( e.g ., once medical certification is returned), PRC will notify the Team Member within five business days of its approval/denial. If a leave is denied, the notice will specify the reason for the ineligibility. Medical or Other Certification for Leave If a leave is requested because of the Team Member’s or a covered family member’s serious health condition, the Team Member must supply appropriate medical certification from a health care provider supporting the need for leave. The medical certification must be provided to PRC prior to the commencement of the leave or within 15 days after it is requested, except in unusual circumstances.
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

text: Medical or Other Certification for Leave If a leave is requested because of the Team Member’s or a covered family member’s serious health condition, the Team Member must supply appropriate medical certification from a health care provider supporting the need for leave. The medical certification must be provided to PRC prior to the commencement of the leave or within 15 days after it is requested, except in unusual circumstances. PRC will inform Team Members if the medical certification submitted is insufficient or incomplete and provide Team Members at least seven calendar days to cure any deficiencies. Failure to provide the medical certification in a timely manner will result in denial or delay of leave until it is provided. Health Care Provider Definition.
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

text: PRC will inform Team Members if the medical certification submitted is insufficient or incomplete and provide Team Members at least seven calendar days to cure any deficiencies. Failure to provide the medical certification in a timely manner will result in denial or delay of leave until it is provided. Health Care Provider Definition. Health care providers who may provide certification of a serious health condition include: doctors of medicine or osteopathy authorized to practice medicine or surgery (as appropriate) by the State in which the doctor practices; podiatrists, dentists, clinical psychologists, optometrists, and chiropractors (limited to treatment consisting of manual manipulation of the spine to correct a subluxation as demonstrated by X-ray to exist) authorized to practice in the State and performing within the scope of their practice under State law; nurse practitioners, nurse-midwives, and clinical social workers authorized to practice under State law and performing within the scope of their practice as defined under State law.
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

Under certain circumstances, PRC, at its expense, may require a second and third medical examination for certification of the Team Member’s own serious health condition.

If additional leave is requested at the end of the period that the health care provider originally estimated the Team Member needed for leave, PRC may require medical recertification.

text: Health care providers who may provide certification of a serious health condition include: doctors of medicine or osteopathy authorized to practice medicine or surgery (as appropriate) by the State in which the doctor practices; podiatrists, dentists, clinical psychologists, optometrists, and chiropractors (limited to treatment consisting of manual manipulation of the spine to correct a subluxation as demonstrated by X-ray to exist) authorized to practice in the State and performing within the scope of their practice under State law; nurse practitioners, nurse-midwives, and clinical social workers authorized to practice under State law and performing within the scope of their practice as defined under State law. Under certain circumstances, PRC, at its expense, may require a second and third medical examination for certification of the Team Member’s own serious health condition. If additional leave is requested at the end of the period that the health care provider originally estimated the Team Member needed for leave, PRC may require medical recertification. The Team Member is responsible for paying for the cost of the recertification.
source: Benefits Pechanga Medical Leave (PML) Policy May 2019.docx

The Team Member is responsible for paying for the cost of the recertification. Any recertification must be provided to PRC prior to the commencement of the additional leave or within 15 days after it is requested. In connection with a request for leave due to a qualifying exigency or military caregiver leave, PRC may also request that a Team Member provide a complete and sufficient certification and/or other documents in support of the leave.

text: Substitution of Paid Leave Team Members must substitute accrued PTO for unpaid PML. For exempt Team Members, this means the hours of PML taken by the Team Member in the workweek will be deducted from the normal 40 workweek hours credited to exempt employees for payroll calculation purposes. Maintenance of Health Benefits Team Members on PML will be allowed to continue receiving regularly provided healthcare benefits, including family coverage, during the leave. text: Original Issue Date :	March 3, 2009 SOP Title : 	PRC ALL Early Out Policy Affected Departments :	PRC ALL
source: PRC ALL Early Out Policy June 2021.docx

text: Policy and Purpose :	The purpose of this policy is to define the criteria for authorized and unauthorized “early outs.” The policy also outlines the process by which early outs will be administered and tracked, in order to maintain fairness and consistency. Placed policy into proper format. Procedure(s): Definition- An “early out” is defined as leaving work before the end of one’s scheduled shift. Authorized ‘Early-out’ : Management allows (or requests) a team member to clock out before the end of their scheduled shift, based on: Level of business; Over coverage of shift; Sick or temporary inability to perform job functions. Unauthorized ‘Early-out’ : If a team member leaves before the end of their scheduled shift without receiving approval from management, they are subject to disciplinary actions, and the action may be considered job abandonment. See the “Performance Management & Standards of Conduct Policy,” for more information.
source: PRC ALL Early Out Policy June 2021.docx

text: Unauthorized ‘Early-out’ : If a team member leaves before the end of their scheduled shift without receiving approval from management, they are subject to disciplinary actions, and the action may be considered job abandonment. See the “Performance Management & Standards of Conduct Policy,” for more information. Full-time team members are regularly scheduled to work a minimum of 30 hours per week, in order to be eligible for full-time benefits. The 30 hour minimum is not a guarantee, and management reserves the right to send team members home early, if business levels are reduced. Administration: Voluntary : If business levels dictate the need, management can ask for volunteers to leave early. The request will be made to all team members on the shift. Early-outs are awarded on a first come, first serve basis.
source: PRC ALL Early Out Policy June 2021.docx

text: If business levels dictate the need, management can ask for volunteers to leave early. The request will be made to all team members on the shift. Early-outs are awarded on a first come, first serve basis. In the cases that demand for voluntary early-outs exceed the availability, the option must be rotated among team members. For use of Paid Time Off (PTO), refer to the “Paid Time Off” Policy. Involuntary : If management requests for volunteers to leave early, and there are no volunteers, management can send one or more team members home. All team members must equally be considered for the involuntary early out, and will be rotated based on the following criteria: Number of hours worked in the current quarter. Team members with the most hours should be considered first to leave. Equal rotation to include all team members on shift.
source: PRC ALL Early Out Policy June 2021.docx

text: Number of hours worked in the current quarter. Team members with the most hours should be considered first to leave. Equal rotation to include all team members on shift. Early out is to be documented by management and must rotate all team members through the process. The rotation process can be determined by the department. Rotation must be applied consistently and uniformly in all areas, to ensure that all team members receive the same treatment. Sick or Temporary Impairment: Team members may request to leave their shift early due to sickness, or temporary inability to perform job duties. It is strongly recommended that a team member consult and EMT before leaving the facility. PTO time should be used by the team member, in order to maintain a 30 hour workweek for benefits eligibility. Management must be notified by the team member of their intention (or need) to early out.
source: PRC ALL Early Out Policy June 2021.docx

text: It is strongly recommended that a team member consult and EMT before leaving the facility. PTO time should be used by the team member, in order to maintain a 30 hour workweek for benefits eligibility. Management must be notified by the team member of their intention (or need) to early out. Unauthorized early outs will be subject to the Attendance Policy, and possible progressive discipline. For more information, refer to the “Attendance Policy.” Documentation: Once a team member has volunteered or been selected to “early-out,” management must document the event. It can be documented in the following ways: Logged in shift reports. Recorded in the team member journal. Recorded in the team member’s file. Documentation must occur in a consistent manner for the department. Documentation needs to have clear methodology, location and shift information.
source: PRC ALL Early Out Policy June 2021.docx

Policy and Purpose :	This policy establishes the guidelines of usage for team members using Social Media accounts on behalf of Pechanga. This policy only applies to those who are using Pechanga created accounts. This policy does not apply to any personal Social Media account a team member may have. The guidelines for personal Social Media accounts are listed in a separate general Social Media Policy. Marketing Guidelines for Requesting Accounts/Pages/Profiles Team Members may not create a profile, page, alias or account on any social network or Web site that in any way represents Pechanga Resort & Casino or any other entity affiliated with Pechanga on property (e.g. Journey, Kelsey’s, etc.). If a Team Member would like to request a profile, page, alias or account be created for a department or entity at Pechanga, please contact the Pechanga Resort & Casino Social Media department.

Original Issue Date :	January 13, 2016 SOP Title : Wellness Incentive Prize Distribution Affected Departments : PRC ALL

Policy and Purpose :	The purpose of this policy is to standardize the process for Distribution of Wellness related raffle prizes. This Standard Operating Procedure provides knowledge and accountability for Management Designee(s) when ordering, receiving and distributing Wellness related prizes and to identify eligibility guidelines for Team Members. Procedures for Prizes: Authorization All Prizes must have approval from HR Benefits Manager, HR/Talent Management Director and/or Vice President HR/Talent Management. Purchase/Receipt/Storage/Distribution of Gift Cards a.   Purchase HR Benefits Manager or HR Executive Assistant will purchase prizes in accordance with the Pechanga Purchasing Procedure. Form of payment may vary. b.   Receipt/Storage Upon delivery to Pechanga, HR Benefits Manager will be notified of shipment by warehouse supervisor. Shipment will be placed in designated location in camera view upon receipt. Shipment will be checked for damage and tampering. Prizes will then be transported to a secure location within HR/TM, counted and logged in.

Shipment will be placed in designated location in camera view upon receipt. Shipment will be checked for damage and tampering. Prizes will then be transported to a secure location within HR/TM, counted and logged in. Discrepancies should be noted and reported. c.   Distribution The following Standard Operating Procedures will be used by HR/management to distribute prizes: The “Sign off Sheet” will be kept up to date and forwarded monthly to HR Benefits. HR Benefits will update Inventory “Prize” sheet and file sign off sheet. Team members who are not present at the time of announcement will forfeit their prize and the next winner will be announced. Winner will need to sign an acknowledgment form confirming receipt of prize. HR Benefits will update Inventory “Prize” sheet and file sign off sheet.

Team members who are not present at the time of announcement will forfeit their prize and the next winner will be announced. Winner will need to sign an acknowledgment form confirming receipt of prize. HR Benefits will update Inventory “Prize” sheet and file sign off sheet. The Department Representative will be given a “sign off” sheet which will include all the team members in the assigned department that won/earned a prize. Department Representative will be responsible for collecting signatures from winners. All prizes that remain unclaimed after two weeks from date of event will be audited, and secured in HR/TM. Prizes will be used as miscellaneous prizes for other programs by the Benefits Department. Occupational Health Prizes Prizes given to Occupational Health to raffle during any OCC Event must have a sign off sheet. Monthly Seminars

Occupational Health Prizes Prizes given to Occupational Health to raffle during any OCC Event must have a sign off sheet. Monthly Seminars Prizes distributed during a monthly seminar will require for the team member to (1) attend the seminar and (2) be present at when the winner is announced to receive the prize. Wellness Challenges Prizes will be distributed to assigned Department Representative. Wellness Events (Health Fair) Prizes distributed during a wellness event will require for the team member to (1) attend the event (2) swipe their badge at the Kiosk to collect their prize. All prizes that remain unclaimed after two weeks from date of event will be audited, and secured in HR/TM. Prizes will be used as miscellaneous prizes for other programs by the Benefits Department. Eligibility Requirements: a. All Active Team Members participating in any wellness event is eligible, including exempt and non-exempt status.
