Original Issue Date :	April 1, 2009 SOP Title :	Dress Code Standards Policy Affected Departments : 	All

Policy and Purpose :	The purpose of this policy is to standardize the HR/Talent Management’s Standard Operating Procedures for dress code standards at Pechanga Resort Casino. This policy provides guidance to all Team Members as to what is appropriate and acceptable to wear to work. This policy affects both uniform and non-uniform Team Members, without exception. (Please type your change comments or explanations of the new policy below) This policy repeals and replaces previously published SOP #13853, dated 6/13/2018. It has been revised in its entirety. Introduction The image that Pechanga Resort Casino (PRC) Team Members present to our guests is critical in keeping with our quality reputation in the marketplace. This policy helps us all provide a consistent professional appearance to our guests and co-workers.

Introduction The image that Pechanga Resort Casino (PRC) Team Members present to our guests is critical in keeping with our quality reputation in the marketplace. This policy helps us all provide a consistent professional appearance to our guests and co-workers. Our appearance reflects on ourselves and PRC and the goal is to ensure that we maintain a positive appearance and not to offend our guests or co-workers. Every one of our Team Members is an ambassador for PRC and therefore accepts the responsibility and ambassadorship of representing ‘Pechanga Pride’. We rely on them to maintain an image consistent with our AAA Four-Diamond and Forbes’ ratings. Uniforms PRC ensures that the uniform is clean and pressed. Team Members will obtain their assigned uniforms from the designated uniform area prior to the beginning of their shift.

Uniforms PRC ensures that the uniform is clean and pressed. Team Members will obtain their assigned uniforms from the designated uniform area prior to the beginning of their shift. Team Members are expected to check for tears and missing buttons to ensure PRC’s flawless Four-Diamond image. Uniform requirements and guidelines will vary among the many departments within PRC, however, these are the minimum personal appearance standards. Outerwear (sweaters, jackets, etc.) must be approved by the department manager and must always conform to departmental guidelines. Team Members need to regularly consult the specific uniform standards for their positions for compliance. Issued uniforms are the property of PRC. All uniforms must be returned upon separation from PRC. Team Members are not allowed to self-alter any part of their issued uniform. If a Team Member returns a uniform that has been self-altered, they are not entitled to a refund.

All uniforms must be returned upon separation from PRC. Team Members are not allowed to self-alter any part of their issued uniform. If a Team Member returns a uniform that has been self-altered, they are not entitled to a refund. Uniforms may be changed at any time as designated by PRC. Team Members who choose to wear their uniforms to and from work must wear the uniform in compliance with all uniform guidelines. Uniformed Team Members are not allowed to wear their issued uniform while off-duty in establishments that could have an adverse representation of PRC, i.e. bars, lounges, etc. This applies to issued uniforms and not clothing such as golf shirts and jackets with PRC logo. The identification badge must not be worn off property or during non-work hours. Departments may prohibit Team Members from wearing uniforms off-site. Non-Uniform Team Members

The identification badge must not be worn off property or during non-work hours. Departments may prohibit Team Members from wearing uniforms off-site. Non-Uniform Team Members PRC Team Members are expected to dress in their business attire unless the day’s tasks require otherwise. They must always present a clean, professional appearance defined in accordance with this policy and meets AAA Four-diamond standards. Everyone is expected to be well-groomed and wear clean clothing, free of holes, tears, or other signs of wear. Acceptable clothing should be conservative and considered appropriate within a professional working environment. Clothing with offensive, inappropriate designs or bearing competitor or other company logos are not allowed. Clothing should not be too revealing. Clothing must be properly tailored, clean, crisp and neatly pressed. Acceptable attires include the following: Suits and sports coats; suits are preferred Business slacks Cotton twill (e.g. Dockers) pants

Suits and sports coats; suits are preferred Business slacks Cotton twill (e.g. Dockers) pants Ties are preferred but not required for positions up to Supervisors Dress and skirt lengths may vary from 2 inches above the kneecap through the ankle bone. Slits, kick pleats, buttons, snaps and closures must not exceed 4 inches above the kneecap Business style blouses, sweaters and twin-sets For Assistant Managers and above, the business professional male attire includes wearing a tie, along with a suit, dress shoes and a collared shirt Exceptions may be made for Team Members that are engaged in work that would be made unsafe by the regular business attire (for example, a manager wearing a tie while working on machinery that prohibits the operator from wearing loose clothing) Never Permitted Clothing Miniskirts, blue jeans, denim clothing, stirrups or leggings of any kind

Exceptions may be made for Team Members that are engaged in work that would be made unsafe by the regular business attire (for example, a manager wearing a tie while working on machinery that prohibits the operator from wearing loose clothing) Never Permitted Clothing Miniskirts, blue jeans, denim clothing, stirrups or leggings of any kind Spaghetti straps, low cut tops or blouses, strapless sundresses or tops T-shirts, polo or golf shirts (unless required for a special event or promotion) Sweatshirts, halter tops, baby T’s, tight or cropped tops Shoes, Hosiery  and Accessories Uniform Team Members: Shoes, socks or hosiery must be worn and be business appropriate. Shoes must be solid black in color, clean and in good condition Platform shoes, backless (mules and slides) and sling-back shoes are not permitted Shoes must be closed toe and closed heel. Food and Beverage Team Members must have shoes with slip resistant soles

Platform shoes, backless (mules and slides) and sling-back shoes are not permitted Shoes must be closed toe and closed heel. Food and Beverage Team Members must have shoes with slip resistant soles Non-Uniform Team Members: Business style dress shoes are required; Casual footwear, such as athletic shoes, deck shoes, sandals, thongs, clogs, casual boots such as UGG styles, are not permitted. Slip resistant soles are recommended for all Team Members. DPS, Valet, Paint Shop, Health Club, Executive and Administrative Offices may vary according to specific job requirements. Safety shoes must be worn in specific departments as required. When entering and exiting the facility, Team Members shall continue to display a four-diamond look. Gaming Commission License Badges The ability to obtain a Pechanga Gaming License badge is a privilege and care must be taken to ensure the badge is clean.

When entering and exiting the facility, Team Members shall continue to display a four-diamond look. Gaming Commission License Badges The ability to obtain a Pechanga Gaming License badge is a privilege and care must be taken to ensure the badge is clean. The badge must be worn at all times on the upper left side of shirts and blouses or uniform while on duty, in an upright and readable position. The badge must be affixed with a standard clip that prevents the picture identification side from rotating to a backwards, non-visible position. Nothing can be affixed or attached to the Gaming Commission License identification badges except for the issued clip that attaches to the uniform or non-uniform clothing. The PRC longevity pin or casino wide authorized promotional buttons on uniforms are acceptable. Jewelry and Accessories Team members may wear tasteful jewelry in moderation.

The PRC longevity pin or casino wide authorized promotional buttons on uniforms are acceptable. Jewelry and Accessories Team members may wear tasteful jewelry in moderation. Noticeable body jewelry on any part of the body other than the ear lobes is not permitted (such as jewelry associated with body piercing). Where job duties present any type of safety risk, jewelry may be prohibited or severely limited. Any methods to conceal any unacceptable piercing such as the use of bandages, filler studs, or colored plugs are not permitted. Plugs for earlobes must be flesh color. Necklaces are permitted, however, they must not be worn outside the uniform for uniformed Team Members. Any leather or fabric belt may be worn provided they are without logos, glitter or wording, and must have a professional looking buckle. Undergarments Team Members are required to wear appropriately fitting undergarments at all times.

Any leather or fabric belt may be worn provided they are without logos, glitter or wording, and must have a professional looking buckle. Undergarments Team Members are required to wear appropriately fitting undergarments at all times. Patterned or colored undergarments that are visible when worn under light-colored uniform or other clothing are not permitted. Undergarments are not permitted to extend past the length of the uniform or clothing. Sunglasses Sunglasses are not permitted in the workplace. Hats, Sun Visors, Headscarves Hats and sun visors may only be worn when issued as part of a required uniform by the Wardrobe Department. The hat or sun visor will be worn with bill forward and squarely on the head. Headscarves are allowed however they cannot cover the neck or face. No bandanas are permitted. Fragrance

Headscarves are allowed however they cannot cover the neck or face. No bandanas are permitted. Fragrance Fragrant products that may be offensive to others should be used in moderation out of concern for others in the workplace. Hair and Make Up Hair should be clean, combed, and neatly trimmed or arranged. Unkempt hair is not permitted. Sideburns, mustaches, and beards should be neatly trimmed. Non-traditional hair colors are not permitted. Make-up should be professional and conservative. Hands and nails should be clean and conservatively manicured. Tattoos and Body Marks Visible tattoos on the face are not permitted. Tattoos in other areas cannot be crude, offensive or cause unusual attention. Tattoos that are not in line with this policy must be concealed. Based on organizational/operation need, specific divisional ‘Tattoos and Body Marks’ policy may vary from general PRC policy. Where applicable, departmental policy takes precedence. Personal Hygiene and Grooming

Based on organizational/operation need, specific divisional ‘Tattoos and Body Marks’ policy may vary from general PRC policy. Where applicable, departmental policy takes precedence. Personal Hygiene and Grooming Team members are expected to practice daily hygiene and good grooming habits as set forth in this policy or applicable departmental policy. Maintain personal cleanliness, including bathing and oral hygiene Use deodorants to minimize body odors Clean and trim fingernails Wash hands with soap, only in designated sinks intended for the purpose Cover nose and mouth when coughing and sneezing with tissue or flexed elbow. Spitting into trash cans or in public locations can cause the spread of germs and is therefore, prohibited. Enforcement Guidelines The Dress Code Standards Policy applies to every Team Member, without exception. These are the minimum global standards, and departmental guidelines may be more restrictive. Where applicable, departmental policy takes precedence.

The Dress Code Standards Policy applies to every Team Member, without exception. These are the minimum global standards, and departmental guidelines may be more restrictive. Where applicable, departmental policy takes precedence. This policy is not intended to address all clothing and attire issues. Department leadership should follow these minimum guidelines when making an appearance policy decision. It is imperative that the determination concludes that the attire represents PRC’s corporate philosophy and provides PRC guests with a Four-Diamond image. Any styles, makeup, or personal grooming practices which draw unusual attention, unfavorable comments from the public, or cause discomfort or safety hazards to co-workers or the public are unacceptable. Team Members will exhibit a Four-Diamond personal appearance standard throughout their employment with PRC. We count on Team Members’ commitment to present their personal best. It is the responsibility of department leadership to ensure that all Team Members strictly adhere to this policy.

Team Members will exhibit a Four-Diamond personal appearance standard throughout their employment with PRC. We count on Team Members’ commitment to present their personal best. It is the responsibility of department leadership to ensure that all Team Members strictly adhere to this policy. The standard shall also be enforced via ‘secret shopper’ who will review and evaluate Team Member’s appearance. Managers or supervisors are expected to inform Team Members when they are violating the dress code. Team Members who are in violation are expected to immediately correct the issue. This may include having to leave work to change clothes. They will not be paid for the time away from work. Repeated violations or violations that have major repercussions may result in disciplinary action being taken up to and including separation.

(Copy Attached) Key – aka “All Other Keys” Shall mean any access card, access key, access key fob, any access badge, or any other item used to engage or disengage an access control locking mechanism, thereby permitting access to any room or area not open to the public, or any room, lockbox, drop box, cabinet, kiosk, or drawer that contains cash, chips, or any items of value. The term “Key” shall not mean VSR Keys , which are separately defined below. (Reg. 1.5.7) Key Access Control System - Shall mean a locking system that is hard-wired, consisting of hardware and software, and stores information in two places, in a computer server and a local control panel. text: Original Issue Date :	06/28/2010 SOP Title :	All Team Member Access to PR&C Activities Affected Departments : 	PRC_All

Policy and Purpose :	Human Resource Standard Operating Procedures for All Team Member Access to PR&C Activities Policy. The purpose of this policy is to standardize an official process for event and venue access, restriction, and conduct relating to any type of wagering or betting. Access to PR&C outlets and events by casino guests is top priority. Revise section A- “f” omit Poker Room and language that states team members may access the poker room, play poker and order food in poker. Add PRC team members are prohibited from gambling on the PR&C premises. Procedure(s): Team Member Access to PR&C Outlets (applies to all those without legitimate business reasons for access). Any and all Lounges and Night Clubs: Team Members may not access Cabaret Lounge, Pool Bar, Eagles Nest or any other bars on PR&C property.

Team Member Access to PR&C Outlets (applies to all those without legitimate business reasons for access). Any and all Lounges and Night Clubs: Team Members may not access Cabaret Lounge, Pool Bar, Eagles Nest or any other bars on PR&C property. Access to the Journey’s End and the Journey’s End & Bar is outlined in paragraph (g). Any and all Restaurants: Team Members may access all restaurants on PR&C property. Team Members must be mindful of peak business hours or other specially designated periods. Peak business hours encompass the beginning of swing shift on Fridays through the end of swing shift on Sunday. In the event of a holiday weekend, peak hours encompass the holiday swing shift. Peak business hours also include all regular holidays in the year and show nights on property when acts are sold out.

Peak business hours encompass the beginning of swing shift on Fridays through the end of swing shift on Sunday. In the event of a holiday weekend, peak hours encompass the holiday swing shift. Peak business hours also include all regular holidays in the year and show nights on property when acts are sold out. Convention groups or Special Events may occasion the issuance of additional short term restrictions. All Team Member shall act responsibly and demonstrate professional behavior at all times. Uniformed Team Members may not access the restaurants listed above at any time, with the exception of the Food Court Outlets described below. Food Court Outlets: Team Members may access the Food Court Outlets to purchase food and bring it back to a designated break room. Hotel, Health Club, Pool, Spa Services, RV Resort:

Food Court Outlets: Team Members may access the Food Court Outlets to purchase food and bring it back to a designated break room. Hotel, Health Club, Pool, Spa Services, RV Resort: Team Members may not access the Health Club, Pool or Spa Services, Hotel or RV Resort during peak periods. The Hotel may be utilized on a case by case basis, pre-approved by the department Vice President. Team Members may access the Spa Services Monday thru Thursday. Retail Stores, Coffee Cart, Gas Station: Team Members may access the gift shops, coffee cart, gas station, gas station convenience store and Baja Express. Team Members may also enjoy a discount on gift store purchases and gas. Team Member Gambling: All Team Members are strictly prohibited from taking part in any type of gambling on PR&C premises.

Team Members may also enjoy a discount on gift store purchases and gas. Team Member Gambling: All Team Members are strictly prohibited from taking part in any type of gambling on PR&C premises. This is intended to include wagering, betting pools, contests made between a Team Member and any other person or group of persons. The Journey and Journey’s End: Team Members may access The Journey and Journey’s End restaurant and bar. Team Members can consume alcohol while they are on the premises during their “off” hours or while attending a “special event”. Team Members should not be in uniform and should   identify themselves as a Team Member. There will be no comps with alcohol and alcohol must be divided on a separate check for payment. Team Members shall not consume alcohol and report to work within an 8 hour period.

Team Members should not be in uniform and should   identify themselves as a Team Member. There will be no comps with alcohol and alcohol must be divided on a separate check for payment. Team Members shall not consume alcohol and report to work within an 8 hour period. All Team Members shall drink responsibly and demonstrate professional behavior at all times while playing golf, or utilizing the Journey’s End and Bar. Bingo: Team Members may not access the Bingo Room for the purposes of playing Bingo or without legitimate business reasons for access. Off Duty Team Members: Off duty team members and their families and friends are encouraged to utilize and enjoy PR&C  outlets being mindful of peak business hours mentioned above, and other restrictions cited above and below such as high demand shows, designated events, boxing, etc.

Bingo: Team Members may not access the Bingo Room for the purposes of playing Bingo or without legitimate business reasons for access. Off Duty Team Members: Off duty team members and their families and friends are encouraged to utilize and enjoy PR&C  outlets being mindful of peak business hours mentioned above, and other restrictions cited above and below such as high demand shows, designated events, boxing, etc. No team member, whether on or off-duty, is excused from the requirement of acting responsibly and demonstrating professional behavior at all times while on PR&C property. Alcohol consumption is prohibited in all venues on PR&C property except The Journey and Journey’s End and Bar. Exceptions Team Member Incentive Programs: Exceptions to Team Member access restrictions and/or the alcohol policy may be granted when part of property-wide team member recognition incentive programs. Individual Basis Exceptions:

Team Member Incentive Programs: Exceptions to Team Member access restrictions and/or the alcohol policy may be granted when part of property-wide team member recognition incentive programs. Individual Basis Exceptions: Department Vice Presidents are happy to consider team member requests for access to PR&C outlets and events on an individual basis. At least seven (7) days prior, the team member shall submit a written request specifying the date, time and outlet or event they wish to access. The Department Vice President must grant permission in writing and has full discretion to deny the request. The team member should bring the written permission when accessing the outlet or event. Display of the PR&C Badge is prohibited at such functions. The PR&C Team Member will advise the servers, attendants, etc., that they are a PR&C Team Member. The Comedy Club:

Display of the PR&C Badge is prohibited at such functions. The PR&C Team Member will advise the servers, attendants, etc., that they are a PR&C Team Member. The Comedy Club: Team Members may attend shows at The Comedy Club but may not consume alcohol. Vendor Dinners: Department Vice Presidents may authorize the consumption of alcohol during vendor sponsored-dinners at PR&C restaurants. No Team Member, regardless of position, is exempt from the requirement to drink responsibly and demonstrate professional behavior at all times during such designated events. Future Programs: PR&C may amend or implement additional exceptions to its Team Member Access policy with written permission of the Pechanga Development Corporation Board. Violation of this policy may result in discipline, up to and including termination. Nothing that follows shall alter or amend the PR&C policies prohibiting alcohol consumption or gaming on PR&C property other than those Exceptions cited above.

text: Original Issue Date :	12/01/2013 SOP Title :	FMLA/ Workers’ Compensation Policy Affected Departments : 	All Departments

Policy and Purpose :	To standardize the administration for Family Medical leave of Absences concurrently with time off due to injuries under the Tribe’s Workers’ Compensation ordinance to ensure fairness and consistency throughout Pechanga Resort & Casino. (Please type your change comments or explanations of the new policy below). Issue a policy to ensure accuracy in the process of administering running FMLA concurrent with Workers’ Compensation leave. When Team Members are injured during the course and scope of their employment at Pechanga Resort & Casino (PR&C), the Tribe will provide benefits in an attempt to cure and relieve the industrial injury and provides Team Members job and health insurance protection under FMLA. Who is eligible for Workers’ Compensation (WC) Team Members of Pechanga Resort & Casino are covered (outside contractors and unpaid volunteers may not be covered). Team Members are covered from the beginning until the end of their shift.

Who is eligible for Workers’ Compensation (WC) Team Members of Pechanga Resort & Casino are covered (outside contractors and unpaid volunteers may not be covered). Team Members are covered from the beginning until the end of their shift. Eligibility Requirements for FMLA/WC leave Team Members are eligible if they have worked for Pechanga Resort & Casino for at least one year and have completed 1,250 hours over the previous 12 months. Team Members, who have a serious health condition that makes them unable to perform their job for an accepted Workers’ Compensation claim and are incapacitated for a period of more than 7 consecutive calendar days, may qualify for FMLA protection under a Workers’ Compensation leave. Type and severity of the injury or illness Injuries that arise directly out of employment or occur during the course and scope of employment are covered. Most job-related illness may be covered.

Type and severity of the injury or illness Injuries that arise directly out of employment or occur during the course and scope of employment are covered. Most job-related illness may be covered. (For example, common colds and flu are not covered). The key is whether your job caused the injury or illness. It is important that immediately following the injury, a Team Member report the event to their direct supervisor. Prompt reporting is the key to prompt benefits. Public Safety Detectives will investigate the incident. If a Team Member needs immediate medical treatment, the EMT’s will refer the Team Member to a medical facility. ALL MEDICAL TREATMENT MUST BE PRE-AUTHOIZED BY EMT/OCC HEALTH OR TRIBAL FIRST (The third party administrator under the Tribe’s Workers’ Compensation ordinance). Leave Entitlement under FMLA/WC Leave

ALL MEDICAL TREATMENT MUST BE PRE-AUTHOIZED BY EMT/OCC HEALTH OR TRIBAL FIRST (The third party administrator under the Tribe’s Workers’ Compensation ordinance). Leave Entitlement under FMLA/WC Leave If a Team Member is unable to work due to a serious health condition and placed on Temporary Total Disability (TTD) status by the Workers’ Compensation medical provider or placed on an intermittent or reduced schedule leave and qualifies for FMLA, the Team Member will receive Workers’ Compensation benefits, and the absence from work due to a work-related injury or illness, if the event qualifies, will count toward a Team Members FMLA entitlement as set forth in the Act. If a Team Member, under Workers’ Compensation leave, is offered temporary modified duty and turns it down or uses PTO in lieu of modified duty, the time off is counted against the Team Members applicable 12-weeks of entitled job-protected leave under FMLA.

Leave Entitlement under FMLA/WC Leave If a Team Member is unable to work due to a serious health condition and placed on Temporary Total Disability (TTD) status by the Workers’ Compensation medical provider or placed on an intermittent or reduced schedule leave and qualifies for FMLA, the Team Member will receive Workers’ Compensation benefits, and the absence from work due to a work-related injury or illness, if the event qualifies, will count toward a Team Members FMLA entitlement as set forth in the Act. If a Team Member, under Workers’ Compensation leave, is offered temporary modified duty and turns it down or uses PTO in lieu of modified duty, the time off is counted against the Team Members applicable 12-weeks of entitled job-protected leave under FMLA. If modified duty is not available, the Team Member will continue to get paid through Workers’ Compensation and the time away from work will be counted against the Team Members applicable 12-weeks of entitled job-protected leave under FMLA.

If a Team Member returns to work on modified duty, time worked will not count towards the Team Members 12-weeks entitlement under FMLA.

Intermittent/Reduced Schedule Leave for an accepted Workers’ Compensation claim

If a Team Member returns to work on modified duty, time worked will not count towards the Team Members 12-weeks entitlement under FMLA. Intermittent/Reduced Schedule Leave for an accepted Workers’ Compensation claim FMLA/WC Leave because of a serious health condition may be taken intermittently/reduced leave schedule (reducing the usual number of hours a Team Member works per workweek or workday) if medically necessary, (with the exception of periodic doctor’s appointments during a team members normal work schedule, for an accepted Workers’ Compensation claim). The work status note must be provided to the Occupational Health Department prior to the commencement of a reduced schedule.

Team Members are required to make a reasonable effort to schedule medical treatment so as not to disrupt unduly the operations of PR&C, subject to the approval of their health care provider. Responsibilities Team Members Responsibility Team Members must provide sufficient information for Pechanga Resort & Casino to determine if the leave may qualify for FMLA/WC protection. Sufficient information may include that the Team Member is TTD for a work injury. Team Members also must inform Occupational Health if the requested leave is for a reason for which FMLA leave was previously taken or certified. Team Members will be required to provide a work status note after each visit with the Workers’ Compensation medical provider. Pechanga Resort & Casino’s Responsibility

Team Members also must inform Occupational Health if the requested leave is for a reason for which FMLA leave was previously taken or certified. Team Members will be required to provide a work status note after each visit with the Workers’ Compensation medical provider. Pechanga Resort & Casino’s Responsibility Pechanga Resort & Casino will inform Team Members whether they are eligible for FMLA protection during their WC leave. If they are, the notice will specify if the leave has been designated as FMLA/WC leave, and any additional information required as well as the Team Members rights and responsibilities. If Pechanga Resort & Casino determines that the Team Member is not eligible or that the leave is not FMLA protected, Pechanga Resort & Casino will notify the Team Member and will provide a reason for ineligibility under FMLA. The Team Member will still qualify for leave under our Workers’ Compensation policy.

The Team Member will still qualify for leave under our Workers’ Compensation policy. Procedures Occupational Health, will meet with the Team Member to discuss the work status and determine whether the Team Member is eligible for FMLA.

The Team Member will be given a preliminary approval for FMLA based on the hours and length of service requirement, however final approval for the leave will be decided once a work status note has been received from the Team Members Workers’ Compensation medical provider. Team Member will be given a checklist that outlines what they are responsible for before and while they are out on a leave of absence. Company property must be turned in. Team Member must provide the Occupational Health Department with an update work status note after each medical appointment.

Team Member will be given a checklist that outlines what they are responsible for before and while they are out on a leave of absence. Company property must be turned in. Team Member must provide the Occupational Health Department with an update work status note after each medical appointment. Team Members are required to continue to pay for their share of their health insurance premiums during an approved FMLA/WC leave. Pechanga Resort & Casino will maintain the Team Members health coverage under our “group health plan” on the same terms as if the Team Member had continued to work. A Team Member must pay his/her portion of the premium during the leave. Group health care coverage may cease for the remainder of the leave if the premium payment is more than thirty (30) days late.

Pechanga Resort & Casino will maintain the Team Members health coverage under our “group health plan” on the same terms as if the Team Member had continued to work. A Team Member must pay his/her portion of the premium during the leave. Group health care coverage may cease for the remainder of the leave if the premium payment is more than thirty (30) days late. If the Team Member’s leave becomes extended due to their medical condition, they must notify Occupational Health immediately. Occupational Health will update the Team Members status in the Infinium system and notify the Team Members department of the decision regarding the Team Members leave. Workers’ Compensation payments will not be paid for the first three (3) days a Team Member is disabled by the authorized treating doctor unless the Team Member is hospitalized as an in-patient or unable to work for more than 14 days.

If the Team Member’s leave becomes extended due to their medical condition, they must notify Occupational Health immediately. Occupational Health will update the Team Members status in the Infinium system and notify the Team Members department of the decision regarding the Team Members leave. Workers’ Compensation payments will not be paid for the first three (3) days a Team Member is disabled by the authorized treating doctor unless the Team Member is hospitalized as an in-patient or unable to work for more than 14 days. In these instances, the “three (3) day waiting period” will be paid. A Team Member may elect to use any accrued PTO time they may have accrued for the first three (3) days of injury only . Return to work The Team Member will be required to present a work status note indicating that they may return to work or return to modified duty.

. Return to work The Team Member will be required to present a work status note indicating that they may return to work or return to modified duty. Team Members failing to provide a release to return to work will not be permitted to resume work until it is provided. Within twenty four (24) hours of the Team Member’s expected return to work date, the Team Member must meet with Occupational Health and provide a release to return to work form completed by the treating physician. Occupational Health will update Infinium with the Team Member’s return to work status and email both the Team Members department as well as the LOA return to work group, PRC_Loa_Returns email addresses.

Team Members failing to provide a release to return to work will not be permitted to resume work until it is provided. Within twenty four (24) hours of the Team Member’s expected return to work date, the Team Member must meet with Occupational Health and provide a release to return to work form completed by the treating physician. Occupational Health will update Infinium with the Team Member’s return to work status and email both the Team Members department as well as the LOA return to work group, PRC_Loa_Returns email addresses. Should a Team Member fail to return to work at the end of their approved leave or fail to provide the required documentation for additional leave, the Team Member's employment will be considered job abandonment and accepted as a voluntary resignation.

Within twenty four (24) hours of the Team Member’s expected return to work date, the Team Member must meet with Occupational Health and provide a release to return to work form completed by the treating physician. Occupational Health will update Infinium with the Team Member’s return to work status and email both the Team Members department as well as the LOA return to work group, PRC_Loa_Returns email addresses. Should a Team Member fail to return to work at the end of their approved leave or fail to provide the required documentation for additional leave, the Team Member's employment will be considered job abandonment and accepted as a voluntary resignation. A Team Member that returns to work at any time after the expiration of a leave is required to reimburse PR&C for its share of the health insurance premiums. Legal Compliance

Should a Team Member fail to return to work at the end of their approved leave or fail to provide the required documentation for additional leave, the Team Member's employment will be considered job abandonment and accepted as a voluntary resignation. A Team Member that returns to work at any time after the expiration of a leave is required to reimburse PR&C for its share of the health insurance premiums. Legal Compliance If a Team Member misrepresents facts in order to be granted a leave of absence, he/she may be subject to disciplinary action, up to and including termination of employment. A Team Member may not engage in outside employment or work elsewhere during a Workers’ Compensation leave of absence if such other employment is inconsistent with the work status note or restrictions submitted by the Team Member.

Legal Compliance If a Team Member misrepresents facts in order to be granted a leave of absence, he/she may be subject to disciplinary action, up to and including termination of employment. A Team Member may not engage in outside employment or work elsewhere during a Workers’ Compensation leave of absence if such other employment is inconsistent with the work status note or restrictions submitted by the Team Member. Team Members considering engaging in other employment during a leave, including self-employment, must notify Occupational Health in advance of engaging in any such employment. Failure to comply with any of these provisions may result in disciplinary action, up to and including termination. Pechanga Resort & Casino is owned and operated by a federally recognized Indian tribe and the Casino is on reservation property.

Team Members considering engaging in other employment during a leave, including self-employment, must notify Occupational Health in advance of engaging in any such employment. Failure to comply with any of these provisions may result in disciplinary action, up to and including termination. Pechanga Resort & Casino is owned and operated by a federally recognized Indian tribe and the Casino is on reservation property. Consequently, PR&C is exempt from California labor and employment laws and is not expressly covered by a number of federal labor/employment statutes. The FMLA/WC guidelines set out above are voluntarily adopted by Pechanga Resort & Casino and are not an admission that the FMLA applies at PR&C.
Original Issue Date :	March 11, 2014 SOP Title :	Talent Acquisition: Recruitment and Job Posting Policy Affected Departments : PRC ALL
source: Talent Acquisition Job Posting Policy July 2022.docx

text: Policy and Purpose :	Human Resources Standard Operating Policy and Procedures for Recruitment and Job Posting Guidelines. The purpose of this policy is to standardize an official process for hiring Team Members. It is intended to provide knowledge and accountability for all Team Members who are subject to this policy. This policy has been revised and updated July 18, 2022. New Recruitment and Replacement Job Posting Guidelines Each department manager, director, or designee will be responsible for the task of initializing a job requisition to replace a Team Member. All necessary signatures will then be obtained for the approval of the request before it is sent to the HR/TM Talent Acquisition Department for posting. All new positions must be approved by the General Manager and Vice President of HR/Talent Management prior to its posting. The department manager will submit their request through the HR/TM Talent Acquisition Department.
source: Talent Acquisition Job Posting Policy July 2022.docx

text: All necessary signatures will then be obtained for the approval of the request before it is sent to the HR/TM Talent Acquisition Department for posting. All new positions must be approved by the General Manager and Vice President of HR/Talent Management prior to its posting. The department manager will submit their request through the HR/TM Talent Acquisition Department. Upon approval and receipt of the job requisition form, a Sr. Talent Acquisition Partner will post the position for the minimum amount of time according to the level of the position being posted. All positions will be posted on the Company’s electronic recruiting platform (ATS). The department leadership will authorize outside advertising for specified positions. The Director of HR Operations and the Vice President of HR/Talent Management may exercise the discretion to use professional recruiters for any specific position as deemed necessary.
source: Talent Acquisition Job Posting Policy July 2022.docx

text: All positions will be posted on the Company’s electronic recruiting platform (ATS). The department leadership will authorize outside advertising for specified positions. The Director of HR Operations and the Vice President of HR/Talent Management may exercise the discretion to use professional recruiters for any specific position as deemed necessary. The Director of HR Operations and the Vice President of HR/Talent Management may exercise the discretion to upgrade/downgrade a position while in the recruitment process based on the candidates’ qualifications and expertise. All positions will be posted to the Pechanga Tribe. Exceptions If an open position is designated for internal career advancement or promotion based on exceptional job performance and/or the job duties have changed significantly enough to warrant a position title change, or where there is a correction to the title, normal posting requirements may be waived by the Vice President of HR/Talent Management.
source: Talent Acquisition Job Posting Policy July 2022.docx

text: All positions will be posted to the Pechanga Tribe. Exceptions If an open position is designated for internal career advancement or promotion based on exceptional job performance and/or the job duties have changed significantly enough to warrant a position title change, or where there is a correction to the title, normal posting requirements may be waived by the Vice President of HR/Talent Management. If a Team Member is laid off due to a position elimination or lack of work, an available position’s posting requirements may be waived by the Vice President of HR/Talent Management if such Team Member is qualified to fill the open position. If a Team Member is identified through a Succession Planning Program, job posting requirements may be waived by the Vice President of HR/Talent Management.
source: Talent Acquisition Job Posting Policy July 2022.docx

text: If an open position is designated for internal career advancement or promotion based on exceptional job performance and/or the job duties have changed significantly enough to warrant a position title change, or where there is a correction to the title, normal posting requirements may be waived by the Vice President of HR/Talent Management. If a Team Member is laid off due to a position elimination or lack of work, an available position’s posting requirements may be waived by the Vice President of HR/Talent Management if such Team Member is qualified to fill the open position. If a Team Member is identified through a Succession Planning Program, job posting requirements may be waived by the Vice President of HR/Talent Management. If a Team Member fails their Probationary Period in a new position and is deemed qualified for another role within the same division, job posting requirements may be waived by the Vice President of HR/Talent Management.
source: Talent Acquisition Job Posting Policy July 2022.docx

If a Team Member fails their Probationary Period in a new position and is deemed qualified for another role within the same division, job posting requirements may be waived by the Vice President of HR/Talent Management. If a Team Member fails their Probationary Period in a new position, and would like to return to their previous position, job posting requirements may be waived by the Vice President of HR/Talent Management, contingent on approval of a “meeting expectations” on the TM’s most recent performance appraisal, and approval of the divisional vice president.
source: Talent Acquisition Job Posting Policy July 2022.docx

If a candidate has been offered a position and then counters, the VP of HR/Talent Management may exercise discretion to upgrade the position and/or salary during negotiations, if it is in alignment with business operations.

If a candidate has been offered a position and then counters, the VP of HR/Talent Management may exercise discretion to upgrade the position and/or salary during negotiations, if it is in alignment with business operations. Recruitment and Duration of Postings The job announcement may have an ‘open until filled’ closing date if approved by the department.
source: Talent Acquisition Job Posting Policy July 2022.docx

text: If a candidate has been offered a position and then counters, the VP of HR/Talent Management may exercise discretion to upgrade the position and/or salary during negotiations, if it is in alignment with business operations. Recruitment and Duration of Postings The job announcement may have an ‘open until filled’ closing date if approved by the department. However, the announcement cannot be posted fewer days than is outlined. Posting requirements can run concurrently with posting to the Pechanga tribe, internal candidates, and external recruiters. The posting guidelines for various levels of jobs are as follows: Once posted for the minimum ten (10) business days, a recruitment can be paused while candidates are sourced. Once a candidate is identified, the recruitment can reopen under the original post, provided it is within 90-days of the original post.
source: Talent Acquisition Job Posting Policy July 2022.docx

text: The posting guidelines for various levels of jobs are as follows: Once posted for the minimum ten (10) business days, a recruitment can be paused while candidates are sourced. Once a candidate is identified, the recruitment can reopen under the original post, provided it is within 90-days of the original post. All approved new and pre-existing postings must be posted to the Pechanga tribe as well as internally and may be externally posted for a minimum of ten (10) business days.
source: Talent Acquisition Job Posting Policy July 2022.docx

text: Original Issue Date :	September 24, 2008 SOP Title :	Team Member Rehire Policy Affected Departments : 	PRC_ALL

Policy and Purpose :	Human Resource Standard Operating Procedures for the Team Member Rehire Policy. The purpose of this policy is to standardize an official process for rehiring former Team Members. -Add Bridging of Service Language Authorization: The rehire of Team Members is contingent upon having had a positive work record. A former Team Member whose employment was involuntarily separated for wrongdoing or misconduct; for violating PR&C rules/policies, or who resigned in lieu of separation for such reasons is ineligible for re-employment with PR&C. The following is a list of prerequisites that must be met to be considered eligible for rehire: Performance evaluations reflecting that the Team Member’s performance consistently met the requirements of the job. Successful completion of the original introductory period; Procedure(s): A former team member who is seeking re-employment must indicate his/her prior employment with PR&C on the job application.

Successful completion of the original introductory period; Procedure(s): A former team member who is seeking re-employment must indicate his/her prior employment with PR&C on the job application. Failure to do so will result in ineligibility for re-employment regardless of the reason for separation. All applications will be subject to an HR/Talent Management review of employment records to determine previous employment status, reason for separation of employment and eligibility for rehire. Applicants ineligible for rehire per this policy will be notified by HR/Talent Management. Rehired Team Members will be assigned their previous badge number, however, seniority will not be bridged. The rehired team member will be considered a new hire for any matters relating to seniority, such as but not limited to, benefits eligibility and vacation accrual. Team Members may qualify to have their prior service bridged for the purpose of Service Award Events only .

Team Members may qualify to have their prior service bridged for the purpose of Service Award Events only . Requests for bridging of service will be forwarded to the Pechanga Development Corporation Board for decision and such decisions will be made on a case-by- case basis. Issues that will be considered: How long the break in service was How long the Team Member worked at Pechanga Whether the Team Member left on good terms No disciplinary actions on file Service Bridging would only be used for attendance at Service Awards events All Team Members who have been involuntary separated from PR&C will be banned for a minimum of 3 months from property and are not eligible for rehire during this time. The Human Resources Department will review all previous separations for eligibility in the re-hiring process. Rehire of Laid Off Team Members Re-hiring:

The Human Resources Department will review all previous separations for eligibility in the re-hiring process. Rehire of Laid Off Team Members Re-hiring: All Team Members subject to a reduction in force shall be considered for re-hire subject to existing personnel policies, procedures and SOPs including past performance, length of service, critical skills, and guest service demands. Adjusted Hire Date: Any Team Member separated as a result of a Reduction in Force (RIF) and rehired within 90 days of the RIF separation date will have their length of service bridged for the purpose of vacation accrual and any other seniority-based benefit. This will not apply to shift scheduling or bidding. Their adjusted hire date will not include time away from PR&C, but will take into account their original hire date.

text: Policy and Purpose :	To provide a structured process to support Team Member success and to address performance and conduct issues Repeals and Replaces: Progressive Discipline Standard Rules of Conduct At Will Policy A.	Policy PRC manages proactively to support Team Member success and address performance and conduct issues. Performance management steps are usually taken in sequence when a Team Member exhibits performance or conduct issues. However, depending on the situation, any step may be repeated, omitted, or taken out of sequence. PRC reserves the right to advance directly to a higher step of performance management, up to and including separation, where the circumstances warrant such action. Each situation is considered on an individual basis. B.	Scope This policy applies to all PRC employees who have successfully completed their introductory period. C.	Grounds for Performance Management Action

All Performance Management Action forms and related evidence must be provided to Human Resources for recordkeeping purposes. Engaging Human Resources.

Supervisors must engage Human Resources prior to issuing a Third and Final Warning, demotion, or separation. Paid Administrative Leave. A Team Member may be placed on paid administrative leave at any point in the performance management process if PRC determines, in its sole discretion, that the Team Member’s continued presence in the workplace poses a threat to the safety, security, or well-being of any person or entity, or would be disruptive to business operations.

text: Performance Management Decisions. In cases where Human Resources has been engaged to assist in a performance management action, Human Resources will recommend appropriate performance management action in accordance with this Policy. The Team Member’s Department will decide which performance management action to take based on Human Resources’ recommendation. It is the responsibility of Human Resources to ensure that the Department’s decision complies with this Policy and other relevant Human Resources policies. Human Resources has the authority to prohibit a Department from taking a non-compliant performance management action. When a Decision Is Made, a Performance Management Meeting Will Be Held. When a decision is made regarding a performance management action, the designated representative from the Team Member’s Department will meet personally with the Team Member to deliver the action.

text: Human Resources has the authority to prohibit a Department from taking a non-compliant performance management action. When a Decision Is Made, a Performance Management Meeting Will Be Held. When a decision is made regarding a performance management action, the designated representative from the Team Member’s Department will meet personally with the Team Member to deliver the action. The Department’s representative will explain the reason(s) for the action, explain any corrective measures required of the Team Member, address any questions the Team Member may have, and ask the Team Member to sign any associated document to acknowledge receipt. If the Team Member refuses to sign, the Department’s representative will note the Team Member’s refusal on the document. A copy of the final document will be given to the Team Member, and the original will be placed in the Team Member’s performance management file.

text: Original Issue Date :	February 13, 2009 SOP Title :	Time Away From Work Policy Affected Departments : 	PRC All

Policy and Purpose :	Standard Operating Procedures for Time Away From Work Procedure. The purpose of this policy is to standardize the process of Time Away From Work Procedure. It is intended to provide insight, order, and accountability for all employees subject to such policies in Time Away From Work Procedure. Bereavement Leave Paid Time Off: In an effort to provide flexibility to Team Members for the use of time off, Pechanga Resort & Casino combined paid time off in the form of (PTO). PTO can be used for vacation, sick, floating holidays, or personal time off. It is not necessary to distinguish how the time is being used (i.e., vacation, sick or holiday). For clarification, refer to the “Paid Time Off” Policy. Bereavement Leave: B.	Bereavement Leave: 1.	Policy

Bereavement Leave: B.	Bereavement Leave: 1.	Policy Team Members may take three (3) to five (5) days of bereavement leave for the death of an immediate family member. 2.	Timing Bereavement leave may be taken in increments, but all leave must be completed within 90 days of the death giving rise to the need for leave (e.g., following the death of an immediate family member, a Team Member could take three days of bereavement leave immediately to grieve, then two days of bereavement leave for funeral services 40 days later). 3.	Pay Bereavement leave is paid for full-time Team Members who have completed their introductory period, according to the schedule below. It is unpaid for all other Team Members, regardless of introductory period status. Team Members receiving unpaid bereavement leave may concurrently use available PTO.

Bereavement leave is paid for full-time Team Members who have completed their introductory period, according to the schedule below. It is unpaid for all other Team Members, regardless of introductory period status. Team Members receiving unpaid bereavement leave may concurrently use available PTO. Up to three (3) paid days and two (2) unpaid days off for funerals 299 miles or less from PRC or for mourning time not involving travel; and Up to five (5) paid days off for funerals located more than 300 miles from PRC. Bereavement pay is calculated based on the base pay rate at the time of absence and will not include any special forms of compensation, such as tips, incentives, commissions, bonuses, overtime, or shift differentials. 4.	Definitions

Up to five (5) paid days off for funerals located more than 300 miles from PRC. Bereavement pay is calculated based on the base pay rate at the time of absence and will not include any special forms of compensation, such as tips, incentives, commissions, bonuses, overtime, or shift differentials. 4.	Definitions a.	“Immediate Family Member” means the Team Member’s spouse, domestic partner, child, grandchild, parent, grandparent, or sibling; including such family members who are step-relatives, in-laws, or relatives through domestic partnership. 5.	Verification to Immediate Supervisor Team Members using bereavement leave must provide verification (e.g., obituary or death certificate) to the HR Benefits team upon returning to work. Jury Duty & Court Appearance: Jury Duty Time off will be granted to full-time Team Members selected to perform jury duty. Team Members selected for jury duty must immediately notify their Supervisor upon receiving the summons for jury duty.

Jury Duty Time off will be granted to full-time Team Members selected to perform jury duty. Team Members selected for jury duty must immediately notify their Supervisor upon receiving the summons for jury duty. Team Members will be reimbursed for the difference between pay received for jury duty and their normal straight time rate of pay for up to a maximum of 10 days of jury duty, regardless of shift. PR&C will not reimburse Team Members who perform jury duty on their off day. Team Members must submit a copy of the jury summons, court receipt and copies of checks received for jury duty to the Payroll Department for reimbursement.

Team Members will be reimbursed for the difference between pay received for jury duty and their normal straight time rate of pay for up to a maximum of 10 days of jury duty, regardless of shift. PR&C will not reimburse Team Members who perform jury duty on their off day. Team Members must submit a copy of the jury summons, court receipt and copies of checks received for jury duty to the Payroll Department for reimbursement. The salary of exempt Team Members will not be reduced for any week in which they perform any work and also serve on a jury, but will be offset by any amounts received by the Team Member as jury duty fees. When on a jury, Team Members must report for work whenever their presence is not required at court, including during “phone in” or “on call” status.

Team Members must submit a copy of the jury summons, court receipt and copies of checks received for jury duty to the Payroll Department for reimbursement. The salary of exempt Team Members will not be reduced for any week in which they perform any work and also serve on a jury, but will be offset by any amounts received by the Team Member as jury duty fees. When on a jury, Team Members must report for work whenever their presence is not required at court, including during “phone in” or “on call” status. Team Members who cannot report to work due to jury duty may be required to show proof of jury service or appearance. Business Related Court Appearances

When on a jury, Team Members must report for work whenever their presence is not required at court, including during “phone in” or “on call” status. Team Members who cannot report to work due to jury duty may be required to show proof of jury service or appearance. Business Related Court Appearances Team Members who are subpoenaed by PR&C to appear in court as a witness, or subpoenaed for an employment-related matter, are paid their normal straight time rate of pay upon submission of the summons or subpoena to their supervisor. Personal Court Appearances Team Members who appear in court on their own behalf or who are subpoenaed for a non-work related matter must use personal leave or PTO leave. Military Leave: Pechanga Resort & Casino is committed to protecting the job rights of Team Members absent on military leave.

Team Members who appear in court on their own behalf or who are subpoenaed for a non-work related matter must use personal leave or PTO leave. Military Leave: Pechanga Resort & Casino is committed to protecting the job rights of Team Members absent on military leave. In accordance with federal law, it is the Company’s policy that no Team Member or prospective Team Member will be subjected to any form of discrimination on the basis of that person's membership in or obligation to perform service for any of the Uniformed Services of the United States. Specifically, no person will be denied employment, reemployment, promotion, or other benefit of employment on the basis of such membership. Eligibility Team Members taking part in a variety of military duties are eligible for benefits under this policy.

Specifically, no person will be denied employment, reemployment, promotion, or other benefit of employment on the basis of such membership. Eligibility Team Members taking part in a variety of military duties are eligible for benefits under this policy. Such military duties include leaves of absence taken by members of the uniformed services, including Reservists, National Guard members for training, periods of active military service, and funeral honors duty, as well as time spent being examined to determine fitness to perform such service. Subject to certain exceptions under the applicable laws. Leave Type Training Team Members will be granted time off with pay for annual training duty provided the duty is obligatory to maintain military status. Team Members will be paid the difference in the amount they receive from the U.S. Government for this training and their regular wages for that period.

Training Team Members will be granted time off with pay for annual training duty provided the duty is obligatory to maintain military status. Team Members will be paid the difference in the amount they receive from the U.S. Government for this training and their regular wages for that period. The maximum payment is for two (2) weeks or eighty (80) hours. Military pay does not include reimbursement for travel allowance. Any period of time spent on military duty in excess of the maximum paid time allowed may be taken as "Paid Time Off” (PTO) or a personal leave without pay including a Reservist reporting one weekend a month. Active Duty 90 days or less of military service – a team member will remain in an active status with PR&C and will be reinstated to a position that he or she would have attained if employment had not been interrupted.

Active Duty 90 days or less of military service – a team member will remain in an active status with PR&C and will be reinstated to a position that he or she would have attained if employment had not been interrupted. More than 91 days and less than 5 years of military service – team member will be processed as “INACTIVE” for system purposes and will have the option of requesting reemployment to a position that he or she would have attained if employment had not been interrupted by military service or a position of like seniority, status and pay, the duties of which the team member is qualified to perform. Notification Team Members submitting the time off request for annual military duty should do so at least two (2) weeks, whenever possible, in advance of the duty.

– team member will be processed as “INACTIVE” for system purposes and will have the option of requesting reemployment to a position that he or she would have attained if employment had not been interrupted by military service or a position of like seniority, status and pay, the duties of which the team member is qualified to perform. Notification Team Members submitting the time off request for annual military duty should do so at least two (2) weeks, whenever possible, in advance of the duty. The request should include the dates of departure and return, and must be accompanied by a copy of the official orders upon receipt from the U.S. Government. Procedure Unless military necessity prevents it, or is otherwise impossible or unreasonable, a Team Member should provide PR&C with notice of the need for leave as far in advance as is reasonable under the circumstances.

The request should include the dates of departure and return, and must be accompanied by a copy of the official orders upon receipt from the U.S. Government. Procedure Unless military necessity prevents it, or is otherwise impossible or unreasonable, a Team Member should provide PR&C with notice of the need for leave as far in advance as is reasonable under the circumstances. Written notice is preferred, but not required under the law or this policy. To request a temporary or extended military leave of absence, the Team Member should meet with their assigned Benefits Specialist and request a Military leave of absence. The Benefits Department will review and sign the request for leave of absence form and collect any applicable company property. Team Members on temporary or extended military leave may, at their option, use any or all accrued paid time off (PTO) during their absence.

To request a temporary or extended military leave of absence, the Team Member should meet with their assigned Benefits Specialist and request a Military leave of absence. The Benefits Department will review and sign the request for leave of absence form and collect any applicable company property. Team Members on temporary or extended military leave may, at their option, use any or all accrued paid time off (PTO) during their absence. If the Team Member does not return to work, the supervisor must notify the Benefits Department so that appropriate action may be taken. 5. Benefits If a Team Member is absent from work due to military service, benefits will continue as follows: A Team Member on extended military leave may elect to continue group health insurance coverage for the Team Member and covered dependents under the same terms and conditions for a period not to exceed 31 days

If a Team Member is absent from work due to military service, benefits will continue as follows: A Team Member on extended military leave may elect to continue group health insurance coverage for the Team Member and covered dependents under the same terms and conditions for a period not to exceed 31 days from the date the military leave of absence begins. The Team Member must pay, per pay period, the premium normally paid by the Team Member. After the initial 31 day period, the Team Member and covered dependents can continue group health insurance (under COBRA) up to 24 months at 102% of the overall (both employer and employee) premium rate. The group life insurance, AD&D, disability, legal, auto and pet insurance provided by PR&C will terminate the day the Team Member becomes active military .

The group life insurance, AD&D, disability, legal, auto and pet insurance provided by PR&C will terminate the day the Team Member becomes active military . Team Members do not accrue paid time off (PTO) leave while on military leave of absence status. With respect to the PR&C’s retirement plan, upon reemployment, Team Members who have taken military leave will be credited for purposes of vesting with the time spent in military service and will be treated as not having incurred a break in service. Immediately upon reemployment, the Team Member may, at the Team Member’s election , make any or all Team Member contributions that the Team Member would have been eligible to make had the Team Member's employment not been interrupted by military service.

Such contributions must be made within a period that begins with the Team Member's reemployment and that is not greater in duration than three times the length of the Team Member's military service. Team Members will receive all associated company match for such contributions. Team members will have their health insurance coverage (medical, dental and vision) including basic life and 401k reinstated upon their return to work date with no waiting period. Reemployment Upon a Team Member's prompt application for reemployment (as defined below), a Team Member will be reinstated to employment in the following manner depending upon the Team Member's period of military service: For periods of service of up to 30 consecutive days, the team member must report back to work for the first full regularly scheduled work day following the completion of the period of service and transportation home, plus an 8-hour period for rest. If reporting back within this deadline is “impossible or unreasonable” through no fault of the team member, he or she must report back as soon as possible after the expiration of the 8-hour resting period. After a period of service of 31 – 180 Days,

the team member must report back to work for the first full regularly scheduled work day following the completion of the period of service and transportation home, plus an 8-hour period for rest. If reporting back within this deadline is “impossible or unreasonable” through no fault of the team member, he or she must report back as soon as possible after the expiration of the 8-hour resting period. After a period of service of 31 – 180 Days, the team member must submit a written or verbal application for reemployment with the employer no later than 14 days after the completion of the period of service. If submitting the application within 14 days is impossible or unreasonable through no fault of the team member, he or she must submit the application as soon as possible thereafter. After a period of service of 181 days or more

the team member must submit a written or verbal application for reemployment with the employer no later than 14 days after the completion of the period of service. If submitting the application within 14 days is impossible or unreasonable through no fault of the team member, he or she must submit the application as soon as possible thereafter. After a period of service of 181 days or more , the team member must submit an application for reemployment no later than 90 days after competition of the period of service. These deadlines to report to work or apply or reemployment can be extended up to two years to accommodate a period during which a team member was hospitalized for or convalescing from an injury or illness that occurred or was aggravated during a period of military service.

General Benefits Upon Reemployment Team Members reemployed following military leave will receive seniority and other benefits determined by seniority that the Team Member had at the beginning of the military leave, plus any additional seniority and benefits the Team Member would have attained, with reasonable certainty, had the individual remained continuously employed. Emergency Responder Leave Pechanga Resort & Casino provides unpaid leave to eligible team members who are volunteer firefighters, reserve peace officers or emergency rescue personnel so that such team members may respond to an emergency duty call, team members may use accrued, unused paid time off for leave taken under this policy. Team Member will be approved for 30 days in a 12-month period for this leave.

Emergency Responder Leave Pechanga Resort & Casino provides unpaid leave to eligible team members who are volunteer firefighters, reserve peace officers or emergency rescue personnel so that such team members may respond to an emergency duty call, team members may use accrued, unused paid time off for leave taken under this policy. Team Member will be approved for 30 days in a 12-month period for this leave. If the need under this leave extends beyond 30 days in a 12-month period, approval for additional leave will be based on business operational needs. Additionally, Pechanga provides unpaid leave, up to 14 days per calendar year, to such team members so that they may engage in scheduled fire, law enforcement or emergency rescue training. For purposes of this policy, ‘volunteer firefighter ” means a person registered as a volunteer member of a regularly organized fire department. An  “ emergency rescue personnel

” means a person registered as a volunteer member of a regularly organized fire department. An  “ emergency rescue personnel ” means any person who is an officer, employee, or member of a fire department or fire protection or firefighting agency of the federal government, the State of California, a city, county, district, or other public or municipal corporation or political subdivision, or of a sheriff’s department, police department, or a private fire department, whether that person is a volunteer or partly paid or fully paid, while he or she is actually engaged in providing emergency services. Team Members Responsibility Team members requesting leave under this policy should comply with the following requirements: As soon as practicable after hire, or on becoming designated as a volunteer firefighter, reserve peace officer or emergency rescue personnel, Team Members should notify their Department Manager and HR Benefits Specialist of their status.

Team Members Responsibility Team members requesting leave under this policy should comply with the following requirements: As soon as practicable after hire, or on becoming designated as a volunteer firefighter, reserve peace officer or emergency rescue personnel, Team Members should notify their Department Manager and HR Benefits Specialist of their status. When called to emergency service or scheduled training, a team member should notify his or her manager/supervisor and HR Benefits Specialist as soon as practicable of the need for leave under this policy and provide information regarding the start and end dates for the requested leave. When returning from leave under this policy, the team member should provide their HR Benefits Specialist with appropriate written documentation confirming that during the leave the team member was actively engaged in responding to an emergency or participating in scheduled training.

Leave of Absence (Non-FMLA) Pechanga Resort & Casino is owned and operated by a federally recognized Indian tribe and the Casino is on reservation property. Consequently, PR&C is exempt from California labor and employment laws and is not expressly covered by a number of federal labor/employment statues. The guidelines set out below are voluntarily adopted by Pechanga Resort & Casino. Should you have questions, please consult with the Human Resources Benefits Department. Team Members Responsibilities Requests Leave of Absence Team Members must provide a 30 day advance notice of the need to take a leave when the need is foreseeable, failure to provide such notice may be grounds for delaying the leave.

Team Members Responsibilities Requests Leave of Absence Team Members must provide a 30 day advance notice of the need to take a leave when the need is foreseeable, failure to provide such notice may be grounds for delaying the leave. When 30 days’ notice is not possible, the Team Member must provide notice as soon as practicable, generally within one (1) to two (2) business days of learning of the need for leave, and must comply with Pechanga Resort & Casino’s normal call-in procedure. Team Members must provide sufficient information to determine if the leave may qualify for protection and the anticipated timing and duration of the leave. Sufficient information may include that the Team Member is unable to perform job functions; the need for hospitalization or continuing treatment by a health care provider, or circumstances supporting the need for military family leave.

When 30 days’ notice is not possible, the Team Member must provide notice as soon as practicable, generally within one (1) to two (2) business days of learning of the need for leave, and must comply with Pechanga Resort & Casino’s normal call-in procedure. Sufficient information may include that the Team Member is unable to perform job functions; the need for hospitalization or continuing treatment by a health care provider, or circumstances supporting the need for military family leave. Team Members also must inform Pechanga Resort & Casino if the requested leave is for a reason for which a leave was previously taken or certified. Team Members will be required to provide a certification supporting the need for leave. Team Member “Call-In” Requirement

Team Members will be required to provide a certification supporting the need for leave. Team Member “Call-In” Requirement When Team Members are unable to work their scheduled shift, they are expected to notify their supervisor at least (2) hours prior to their scheduled start time. Team Members will need to continue to call in each day of their absence until their leave has been approved. If you are unable to comply due to an emergency or other extenuating circumstances you (or a family member) must call as soon as possible.

Medical Certification If a leave is requested because of the Team Member’s serious health condition, the Team Member and the relevant health care provider must supply appropriate medical certification supporting the need for leave. Medical certification which state “Lifetime” restrictions will not be valid.

Medical certification which state “Lifetime” restrictions will not be valid. Medical certifications are required to be re-certified every six (6) months. If the badge is not received, the Team Member will be charged $50.00 (non-refundable). Maintenance of Health Benefits PR&C is required to maintain group health insurance coverage, including family coverage, for a Team Member on an approved leave of absence on the same terms as if the Team Member continued to work. PR&C’s obligation to maintain health benefits under a family medical leave of absence stops if and when the Team Member informs PR&C of an intent not to return to work at the end of the leave period, or if the Team Member fails to return to work when the leave entitlement is exhausted. PR&C’s obligation also stops if the Team Members premium payment is more than 30 days late and PR&C has given the employee written notice at least 15 days in advance advising that coverage will cease if payment is not received.

Pechanga Resort & Casino’s Responsibility Request Received The Benefit Specialist, or in the case of a Workers’ Compensation, Occupational Health, will meet with the Team Member to discuss the request for a leave of absence and determine whether the Team Member is eligible for a leave of absence. The Team Member will receive a copy of their Rights and Responsibilities as well as a preliminary approval/denial of the request for a leave. If PR&C receives incomplete or insufficient information, the leave will be placed on a pending status until the medical certification is received and any clarification needed has been obtained. “A medical certification is considered incomplete if one or more of the applicable entries have not been completed. Designation Notice Once a complete medical certification has been received the Team Member will be notified whether his or her leave request has been approved or denied. If a leave is denied, the notice will specify the reason for the ineligibility. Team Members failing to provide a release to return to work when required to do so will not be permitted to resume work until it is provided. Types of leaves Medical Leave (Non-FMLA) Medical related absences extending beyond seven (7) consecutive days will require the Team Member to apply for a medical leave of absence. Team Members who do not meet the eligibility requirements for an FMLA

the eligibility requirements for an FMLA (1 year of service and 1,250 hours worked within the 12 months prior to the request for a leave.), but have completed ninety (90) days of continuous employment may be granted an unpaid leave of absence for a non-job related medical conditions up to a maximum of thirty (30) calendar days. Medical Leaves (Non-FMLA) may not be taken intermittently. A medical certification completed by a physician certifying that the Team Member is unable to work and cannot perform the essential functions of the job will be required. A medical leave may only be granted once every 12 months. If a Team Member had an approved leave, the 12 month period will be extended by the number of days the Team Member was away from work.
