A medical leave may only be granted once every 12 months. If a Team Member had an approved leave, the 12 month period will be extended by the number of days the Team Member was away from work. If a Team Member exhausted their 12 weeks under PR&C’s FMLA Policy, within the 12 months prior to the request for a new leave, the Team Member will not be eligible for a Medical (Non-FMLA) Leave. Catastrophic Leave Catastrophic leaves may be granted to a Team Member who has exhausted their 12 weeks under PR&C’s FMLA Policy. A catastrophic leave will not be granted for a period longer than thirty (30) calendar days and may not be taken intermittently.

their 12 weeks under PR&C’s FMLA Policy. A catastrophic leave will not be granted for a period longer than thirty (30) calendar days and may not be taken intermittently. A catastrophic leave may only be granted once every 12 months. If a Team Member had an approved leave, the 12 month period will be extended by the number of days the Team Member was away from work. Definition : A catastrophic injury is a severe injury to the spine, spinal cord or brain and may also include the skull or spinal fracture. A catastrophic illness is a severe or or prolonged illness usually considered to be life-threatening or with the threat of serious residual disability or illness requiring prolong hospitalization or recovery. Examples would include coma, cancer, leukemia, heart attack or stroke.

A catastrophic illness is a severe or or prolonged illness usually considered to be life-threatening or with the threat of serious residual disability or illness requiring prolong hospitalization or recovery. Examples would include coma, cancer, leukemia, heart attack or stroke. In addition a catastrophic leave may also be taken when recovering from a major surgery, long-term in nature and required long recuperation periods or due to being admitted to a medical facility. Pechanga Pregnancy Leave (PPL) Team Members may request PPL for a disability related to pregnancy. Team Members are eligible for PPL after completing a period of 90 days of continuous employment with Pechanga Resort & Casino. PPL provides up to four (4) months of leave which may be taken before or after the child’s birth ( consecutive or intermittent ) . Team Members are required to exhaust accrued PTO over 40 hours. Maternity Leave (Non-FMLA)

. Team Members are required to exhaust accrued PTO over 40 hours. Maternity Leave (Non-FMLA) Team Members that do not meet the eligibility requirements under FMLA (1 year of service and 1,250 hours worked within the 12 months prior to the request for a leave.), but have completed 90 days of continuous employment may qualify for a maternity leave after the birth of a child (continuous only). Leaves may be granted for a period from 6 – 8 weeks only for a disability related to the pregnancy (recovery). Personal Leave of Absence Team Members are eligible to request an unpaid personal leave of absence up to 30 days for extenuating personal reasons upon completion of ninety (90) days of continuous employment. The leave of absence request must be submitted directly to the Team Member’s Vice President or authorized management personnel for approval or denial.

Retain his/her former hire date for all purposes, including company, department, and classification.

text: Policy and Purpose :	To provide a process for resolution of Team Member issues through the chain of command and Human Resources Repeals and Replaces: Team Member Dispute Resolution A.    	Resolution Through the Chain of Command PRC has an “open door” policy, which means that a supervisor’s door is always open to good faith Team Member concerns. Team Members are encouraged to bring any good faith concerns about their employment to their immediate supervisors for resolution. Team Members may use this open door policy without fear of retaliation. A Team Member who has a good faith concern must first attempt to resolve the issue through his or her designated departmental chain of command, except where the issue involves a member of that chain of command, in which case, the Team Member may proceed directly to Human Resources for assistance.
source: Resolution of Team Member Issues July 2018.docx

text: Team Members are encouraged to bring any good faith concerns about their employment to their immediate supervisors for resolution. Team Members may use this open door policy without fear of retaliation. A Team Member who has a good faith concern must first attempt to resolve the issue through his or her designated departmental chain of command, except where the issue involves a member of that chain of command, in which case, the Team Member may proceed directly to Human Resources for assistance. Where applicable, the departmental chain of command includes the Team Member’s immediate supervisor, manager, director and Vice President. If the chain of command does not produce a satisfactory resolution of the Team Member’s concern, the Team Member may submit a written complaint to Human Resources Operations as described below. B.	Filing a Complaint With Human Resources
source: Resolution of Team Member Issues July 2018.docx

text: Where applicable, the departmental chain of command includes the Team Member’s immediate supervisor, manager, director and Vice President. If the chain of command does not produce a satisfactory resolution of the Team Member’s concern, the Team Member may submit a written complaint to Human Resources Operations as described below. B.	Filing a Complaint With Human Resources If a Team Member concern cannot be resolved through the chain of command, the Team Member may submit a written complaint to Human Resources Operations. The following information must be included in the complaint: Date of submission Team Member name Team Member department Complete description of the concern (including any supporting facts, witness, and documents) Human Resources Operations will acknowledge receipt of written employee complaints within seven (7) calendar days.
source: Resolution of Team Member Issues July 2018.docx

text: Team Member department Complete description of the concern (including any supporting facts, witness, and documents) Human Resources Operations will acknowledge receipt of written employee complaints within seven (7) calendar days. To maximize the effectiveness of any related investigation and/or corrective action, all complaints should be submitted as soon as possible after the alleged incident giving rise to the complaint. As a general rule, complaints must be submitted no later than 30 calendar days after the alleged incident, except where the complaint involves discrimination, harassment, or retaliation, in which case, the complaint must be submitted no later than one year after the alleged incident. C. Investigations Where a complaint requires investigation, personnel trained and qualified to conduct workplace investigations will promptly and thoroughly investigate the matter in a fair and impartial manner. Investigations will be documented and tracked to ensure reasonable progress and timely closure.
source: Resolution of Team Member Issues July 2018.docx

text: Investigations Where a complaint requires investigation, personnel trained and qualified to conduct workplace investigations will promptly and thoroughly investigate the matter in a fair and impartial manner. Investigations will be documented and tracked to ensure reasonable progress and timely closure. Based on the evidence collected, PRC will reach a reasonable conclusion and take timely appropriate action. If PRC determines that prohibited conduct has occurred, appropriate and effective corrective and remedial action will be taken in accordance with the circumstances involved. PRC will also take appropriate action designed to deter future misconduct. Any Team Member found to have engaged in any form of prohibited conduct will be subject to performance management up to and including separation. The findings of the investigation will be communicated to the complaining Team Member.
source: Resolution of Team Member Issues July 2018.docx

text: PRC will also take appropriate action designed to deter future misconduct. Any Team Member found to have engaged in any form of prohibited conduct will be subject to performance management up to and including separation. The findings of the investigation will be communicated to the complaining Team Member. PRC will also advise the complaining Team Member of any related corrective action, except where doing so would violate the privacy rights of another Team Member. D. Appeals
source: Resolution of Team Member Issues July 2018.docx

text: PRC will also advise the complaining Team Member of any related corrective action, except where doing so would violate the privacy rights of another Team Member. D. Appeals Pursuant to PRC’s Appeal Process Policy, a Team Member who makes an internal complaint of discrimination, harassment, or retaliation may appeal PRC’s final determination regarding that complaint if the Team Member has substantial evidence that: (1) procedural error(s) substantially affected the fairness of the complaint resolution process such that the final determination would have been different if the procedural error(s) did not occur; or (2) PRC’s final determination is clearly contrary to the evidence presented during the investigation of the complaint such that a reasonable person could not have reached the final determination based on the evidence presented. E. Pechanga Employment Claims Act
source: Resolution of Team Member Issues July 2018.docx

text: Pursuant to PRC’s Appeal Process Policy, a Team Member who makes an internal complaint of discrimination, harassment, or retaliation may appeal PRC’s final determination regarding that complaint if the Team Member has substantial evidence that: (1) procedural error(s) substantially affected the fairness of the complaint resolution process such that the final determination would have been different if the procedural error(s) did not occur; or (2) PRC’s final determination is clearly contrary to the evidence presented during the investigation of the complaint such that a reasonable person could not have reached the final determination based on the evidence presented. E. Pechanga Employment Claims Act If a Team Member complaint of discrimination, harassment, or discrimination cannot be resolved through the complaint and appeal processes described above, the Team Member may file a claim with the Pechanga Tribal Court in accordance with the Pechanga Employment Claims Act. F. Confidentiality
source: Resolution of Team Member Issues July 2018.docx

text: If a Team Member complaint of discrimination, harassment, or discrimination cannot be resolved through the complaint and appeal processes described above, the Team Member may file a claim with the Pechanga Tribal Court in accordance with the Pechanga Employment Claims Act. F. Confidentiality Confidentiality will be maintained throughout the complaint and investigation process to the full extent possible, but complete confidentiality cannot be guaranteed. PRC’s need to conduct an investigation, if necessary, and take prompt and appropriate action to address the complaint may require the disclosure of information to individuals with a need to know. G. Non-Retaliation PRC prohibits retaliation against Team Members who submit or cooperate in the investigation of a good faith suggestion, concern, or complaint. If a Team Member experiences or witnesses retaliatory conduct that violates this policy, the Team Member must immediately report the conduct through the chain of command or to Human Resources Operations.
source: Resolution of Team Member Issues July 2018.docx

If a Team Member experiences or witnesses retaliatory conduct that violates this policy, the Team Member must immediately report the conduct through the chain of command or to Human Resources Operations. Team Members found to have engaged in retaliation in violation of this policy will be subject to performance management up to and including separation.

text: Temporary Help/Talent Pool Policy Scope: PRC ALL Policy & Purpose: Temporary help placement policy. The purpose of this policy is to utilize exempt and non-exempt team members to fulfill the temporary staffing needs of varying departments, and provide them the opportunity for additional non-overtime hours. Procedures: Definition-Talent Pool A talent pool will be created to fill PRC’s temporary staffing needs through temporary work assignments offered to current team members of PRC. The talent pool consist of team members who meet one or more of the following conditions: Full/Part-time Team Members who have been employed for a minimum of 90 days Full-time Team Members whose hours have been reduced Part-time Team Members who are seeking additional hours Any team member selected for a temporary assignment will be required to sign, along with the temporary supervisor, a Temporary Position Agreement prior to the commencement of the assignment. Process

Part-time Team Members who are seeking additional hours Any team member selected for a temporary assignment will be required to sign, along with the temporary supervisor, a Temporary Position Agreement prior to the commencement of the assignment. Process To obtain temporary help a departmental manager must complete a Temporary Help Request Form. All requisitions must indicate a start and end date for the assignment, a description of the duties the individual will be performing, pay, the shift to which the team member will be working, and the work week the team member will be required to work. All requests must be approved by the division Director and/or Vice President, signed by the Department Manager, and submitted to HR/Talent Management. Once received, an HR/Talent Management representative will use the current talent pool to attempt to fill the temporary position.

All requisitions must indicate a start and end date for the assignment, a description of the duties the individual will be performing, pay, the shift to which the team member will be working, and the work week the team member will be required to work. All requests must be approved by the division Director and/or Vice President, signed by the Department Manager, and submitted to HR/Talent Management. Once received, an HR/Talent Management representative will use the current talent pool to attempt to fill the temporary position. If filled, the HR/Talent Management representative will contact the appointed department manager and/or supervisor to coordinate the team member’s temporary placement. The team member will be required to adhere to the department’s policies and procedures while working in the specified area in addition to the performance standards indicated on the job duties.

Once received, an HR/Talent Management representative will use the current talent pool to attempt to fill the temporary position. If filled, the HR/Talent Management representative will contact the appointed department manager and/or supervisor to coordinate the team member’s temporary placement. The team member will be required to adhere to the department’s policies and procedures while working in the specified area in addition to the performance standards indicated on the job duties. Failure to comply may result in removal from the assignment and/or progressive disciplinary action up to and including termination. All placements of team members will be on a temporary basis. Cash handling positions are excluded from this program. Selected team members can only work in areas that correspond with their current badge licensing. B Badges can only work in areas that require a B Badge A Badges can work in both A and B badge areas

Selected team members can only work in areas that correspond with their current badge licensing. B Badges can only work in areas that require a B Badge A Badges can work in both A and B badge areas H Badges can work in both B and H badge areas Team members will be required to adhere to the dress code identified in the temporary work agreement. There is no guarantee that if place in the talent pool the team member will receive temporary work. Temporary Placement Log The HR/Talent Management representative will maintain a detailed daily log of running assignments and team members assigned. A placement log will be emailed to Payroll, Gaming and Compliance as individuals as placed to work in the different areas. Pay for Time Work Team members placed on a temporary assignment will be paid at the accepted position’s minimum pay rate.

A placement log will be emailed to Payroll, Gaming and Compliance as individuals as placed to work in the different areas. Pay for Time Work Team members placed on a temporary assignment will be paid at the accepted position’s minimum pay rate. The team member’s compensation will be charged to the temporary department’s budget. Manual Talent Pool time sheets must be filled out daily be the team member and given to their temporary supervisor and/or manager weekly for approval. All manual Talent Pool time sheets must be submitted to payroll and the HR/Talent Management representative no later than the Monday before the pay period ending. Work Hours The hours worked for the temporary work assignment cannot exceed 12 hours within the work day. No double shifts are permitted. Double shifts are defined as working consecutive shifts without a minimum time lapse of 8 hours. . Confidentiality

No double shifts are permitted. Double shifts are defined as working consecutive shifts without a minimum time lapse of 8 hours. . Confidentiality All team members working on a temporary basis will be required at all times to keep confidential and shall not thereafter, either directly or indirectly, disclose or make available to any person or entity any confidential or trade secret information of PRC or of PRC’s customers. Violation of this policy may result in disciplinary action, up to and including termination

text: Policy and Purpose :	Human Resource/TM Standard Operating Procedures for the Team Member Recruitment Incentive Program. The purpose of this policy is to standardize an official process for all Team Members to follow for the ‘Refer and Earn’ Referral Program in recruitment. This is intended as an incentive program to reward Team Members if the referred friend or family member stays with PRC for a pre-determined time frame. (Please type your change comments or explanations of the new policy below) Changes made to allow greater flexibility in applying program SOP to special recruitment efforts requiring different monetary rewards & eligibility timelines for payment. text: Pechanga Resort Casino (PRC) has designed this program intended to provide a procedure for Team Members to refer friends and family members for possible employment. 1 Team Member Eligibility 2 This program is open to all current Team Members of PRC with the exception of Management (Managers & above) and Talent Acquisition Team Members. 3 Refer and Earn Process 4 The referred candidate must put the Team Member’s name on the application as the referring Team Member at the time the application is submitted. If referred applicant is hired and remains employed for the duration of the programs eligibility requirement. , the referring Team Member is eligible for a  reward in the form of a gift/debit card in an amount specified by the program. text: Amount rewarded will be grossed up for the purpose of taxes. 5 Specific Rules and Regulations 6 A referral does not constitute a commitment to interview, influence, hire on the part of PRC, the referring Team Member or any subsidiary, division or affiliate associated with PRC. The referring Team Member must be employed with PRC at the time the referred Team Member successfully completes the eligibility requirements in order to collect the referral reward. text: Original Issue Date :	November 23, 2020 SOP Title :  	Pre-Employment Drug Testing Affected Departments :	PRC All
source: PRC ALL Pre-Employment Drug Testing Policy November 2020.docx

text: Policy and Purpose :	Standard Operating Procedures for Pre-Employment Drug Testing. The purpose of this policy is to standardize the process of Pre-Employment Drug Testing. It is intended to provide insight, order, and accountability for all employees’ subject to such policies in Pre-Employment Drug Testing. (Please type your change comments or explanations of the new policy below). Policy pertains to the new drug testing process for pre-employment. All candidates who have received a conditional written offer of employment will be required to undergo pre-employment testing for commonly abused controlled substances. Procedure(s): Substances Covered by Pre-Employment Testing Candidates will be tested for their use of commonly abused controlled substances, including: Amphetamines, Opiates and Extended Opiates, Cocaine, Methamphetamines, and Phencyclidine (PCP). Pre-employment testing does not extend to alcohol or marijuana/cannabinoids.
source: PRC ALL Pre-Employment Drug Testing Policy November 2020.docx

text: Substances Covered by Pre-Employment Testing Candidates will be tested for their use of commonly abused controlled substances, including: Amphetamines, Opiates and Extended Opiates, Cocaine, Methamphetamines, and Phencyclidine (PCP). Pre-employment testing does not extend to alcohol or marijuana/cannabinoids. However, employed Team Members are prohibited from being under the influence of alcohol or marijuana/cannabinoids while on duty, regardless of work location, and are subject to reasonable suspicion testing, random testing and/or post-accident testing for such substances in accordance with PRC’s Drug and Alcohol Free Workplace policy. It is strictly prohibited for any applicant or Team Member to buy, sell, transfer, possess or solicit marijuana on the Pechanga Indian Reservation. Refusal to Undergo Testing Candidates who refuse to submit to a drug test or who fail to appear for a drug test will have their conditional offer of employment rescinded. Right to Explain Test Results
source: PRC ALL Pre-Employment Drug Testing Policy November 2020.docx

text: Refusal to Undergo Testing Candidates who refuse to submit to a drug test or who fail to appear for a drug test will have their conditional offer of employment rescinded. Right to Explain Test Results All Candidates have the right to meet with the testing laboratory personnel to explain their test results. These discussions shall be considered confidential, except that information disclosed in such tests will be communicated to personnel within Pechanga Resort & Casino or within the Lab who need to know such information in order to make proper decisions regarding the test results or regarding the employment of the individual. Confidentiality Requirements All records concerning test results will be kept in confidential medical files maintained separately from Pechanga Resort & Casino personnel files, and will be disclosed only to authorized persons with a business need to know.

Testing laboratories may conduct testing only for substances included on the disclosure list provided to the individual, and may not conduct general testing related to the medical conditions of the individual which are unrelated to drug use. text: Original Issue Date :	February 28, 2013 SOP Title :	ON CALL POLICY Affected Departments : 	PRC ALL

Policy and Purpose :	Standard Operating Procedures for ON CALL POLICY. The purpose of this policy is to standardize the process of ON CALL POLICY. It is intended to provide insight, order, and accountability for all employees subject to such policies in the ON CALL POLICY. DEFINITIONS: On-call Status : “On-call” means a Team Member who works irregular hours and only when specifically notified to attend work by the assigned department. The department will provide On-call Team Members with a process to obtain their schedule and/or to notify their supervisor of their availability. It is the on-call Team Member’s responsibility to routinely check their schedule. On-call Team Members are not eligible for benefits. On-call Team Members are not eligible for the Employee Assistance Program (EAP). On-call status does not guarantee or provide preference to move to a full-time or a part-time position.

On-call Team Members are not eligible for benefits. On-call Team Members are not eligible for the Employee Assistance Program (EAP). On-call status does not guarantee or provide preference to move to a full-time or a part-time position. On-Call Team Members are responsible for following the Team Member Handbook policies, company policies and other departmental policies. On-Call Team Members must be available for at least 12 shifts on a rolling 30 day calendar period. Six (6) of the twelve (12) available shifts (on a rolling 30 day calendar period) will be weekend shifts. Weekend shifts are defined as Friday at 12:00pm (noon) to Sunday at 11:59pm. Shift Assignment: This occurs when a Team Member agrees to report for a scheduled shift. Assignments are not guaranteed and are not governed by longevity or other employment factors including total hours worked to date. Occurrence:

This occurs when a Team Member agrees to report for a scheduled shift. Assignments are not guaranteed and are not governed by longevity or other employment factors including total hours worked to date. Occurrence: The PR&C Attendance policy defines an occurrence as calling off for a scheduled shift, being late/tardy to a shift, or any unapproved early clock out. All Team Members at PR&C are subject to the terms of the Attendance policy. Mandatory Day: Defined as a pre-designated day during the year when attendance is required. Failure to work a scheduled shift on a mandatory day will result in 5 occurrences. Emergency Shift: On-call Team Members may be contacted with short notice – less than eight (8) hours prior to start of shift and requested to pick up an extra shift. At-Will Employment: All employment with PR&C is at-will.

On-call Team Members may be contacted with short notice – less than eight (8) hours prior to start of shift and requested to pick up an extra shift. At-Will Employment: All employment with PR&C is at-will. PR&C and the Team Member may end the employment relationship at any time for any reason or no reason with or without notice. 1 PROCEDURES: Any time an On-call Team Member is involuntarily sent home for lack of business after reporting to the property for a shift, they will be paid a minimum of two (2) hours or the length of time worked, whichever is longer. On-call Team Members are responsible for providing up to date contact information (current phone number and/or address).Failure to provide updated contact information may result in disciplinary action up to and including separation On-call Team Members are expected to work when shifts are scheduled and perform all duties.

Any time an On-call Team Member is involuntarily sent home for lack of business after reporting to the property for a shift, they will be paid a minimum of two (2) hours or the length of time worked, whichever is longer. On-call Team Members are responsible for providing up to date contact information (current phone number and/or address).Failure to provide updated contact information may result in disciplinary action up to and including separation On-call Team Members are expected to work when shifts are scheduled and perform all duties. Failure to work at least 50% of shifts in a calendar month may result in disciplinary action up to and including separation provided the team member has been offered at least 4 shifts within the month. On-call Team Members will be provided with a designated time each week by the department to receive their upcoming schedule.

On-call Team Members are expected to work when shifts are scheduled and perform all duties. Failure to work at least 50% of shifts in a calendar month may result in disciplinary action up to and including separation provided the team member has been offered at least 4 shifts within the month. On-call Team Members will be provided with a designated time each week by the department to receive their upcoming schedule. The Team Member is responsible for contacting the department at the time designated to find out if they are scheduled for shifts. 2 ATTENDANCE: All Pechanga Team Members are held to the same Attendance Policy and guidelines. Please refer to Attendance Policy #10226 in Policy IQ or the Team Member Handbook.

Non-Fraternization / Personal Relationships Template: Human Resources SOP Non-Regulatory Version: 1 (6/4/2010) Expiration Date: Approvers: Administrators: Global Administrators Only ↓ ; Editors: Viewers: All Advanced Users - Pechanga ↓ ; All Read Only Viewers - Pechanga ↓ ; All Users - Pecahanga ↓ ; General Manager ↓ ; Global Administrators Only ↓ ; Human Resources ↓ ; Internal Audit ↓ ; Commission, Gaming; Pechanga, HR; Pechanga, PDC; Non-regulatory SOPs; Non-Regulatory SOPS; Related Companies; Folders: Division: Department: VP Approval: GM Approval: Approval Date: SOP : Scope: Applies to all Pechanga Resort & Casino (PR&C) Team Members.

Policy and Purpose: The purpose of this policy is to minimize any potential conflict of interest, sexual harassment liability, favoritism or the perception of favoritism. Procedure(s): 1. Non-Fraternization / Personal Relationships Situations of actual or potential conflicts of interest are to be avoided by all Team Members. Personal or romantic involvement may impair a Team Member's ability to exercise good judgment on behalf of PR&C, if it creates an actual or potential conflict of interest. PR&C reserves the right to transfer either of the Team Members involved in a workplace romance. Factors such as work experience, seniority and PR&C needs will influence the decision. PR&C prohibits Team Members from engaging in public displays of affection or romantic liaisons during business hours. PR&C also discourages Team Members from becoming romantically involved with guests, vendors and competitors. 1. Romantic Relationships with Direct Reports

PR&C also discourages Team Members from becoming romantically involved with guests, vendors and competitors. 1. Romantic Relationships with Direct Reports Team Members may not become romantically involved with direct reports and/or subordinate Team Members. These situations can lead to charges of sexual harassment, retaliation or the perception of favoritism. If a Team Member and a  subordinate Team Member are having a romantic relationship, it is the responsibility of both Team Members to disclose  the  relationship  to  their  Supervisor  and  HR/Talent  Management. PR&C  reserves  the  right  to  take disciplinary action or transfer one or all of the individuals involved. Team  Members  who  become  personally  involved  with  co-workers  should  be  aware  that  serious  risks  and consequences  can  develop  as  a  result  of  the  relationship's  effect  on  business  matters.

If a Team Member and a  subordinate Team Member are having a romantic relationship, it is the responsibility of both Team Members to disclose  the  relationship  to  their  Supervisor  and  HR/Talent  Management. PR&C  reserves  the  right  to  take disciplinary action or transfer one or all of the individuals involved. Team  Members  who  become  personally  involved  with  co-workers  should  be  aware  that  serious  risks  and consequences  can  develop  as  a  result  of  the  relationship's  effect  on  business  matters. PR&C  may  intervene  by discussing  the  issue  with  affected Team  Members  or  taking  appropriate  measures  when,  in  PR&C's  opinion,  it  is necessary to do so to maintain the integrity of work relationships. Team Members who feel that they have been adversely affected by a romantic work relationship are urged to report it immediately to the HR/Talent Management  Department. An investigation will be launched quickly and confidentially. Original Policy Date: 2/16/2010 Version Number: 1

source: PDC  NEW Meal and Rest period August 2016.docx

text: Policy and Purpose :	Standard Operating Procedures for the Meal and Rest Period Policy. The purpose of this policy is to define the Meal and Rest Period Policy and establish guidelines for this process. The team member is unable to leave his/her workstation to take a meal period because of an emergency. Meal and Rest Period Guidelines Team Members who are scheduled to work a period of more than five (5) hours are provided with a minimum 30-minute meal break. Team Members must clock out for their meal break as the time taken is unpaid. However, if the shift is going to end at completion of the sixth (6 th ) hour, no meal period is required. Managers are responsible to schedule team members to take their meal period as close to mid shift as possible however, not prior to the fourth hour.
source: PDC  NEW Meal and Rest period August 2016.docx

text: th ) hour, no meal period is required. Managers are responsible to schedule team members to take their meal period as close to mid shift as possible however, not prior to the fourth hour. Team Members are also allowed a 15-minute rest period for every four (4) hours of work. The break periods do not require Team Members to clock out as these breaks are paid. Managers are responsible for scheduling all meal and rest periods and ensure that all applicable meal and rest periods are taken each shift. Note that rest periods are paid while meal periods are unpaid. Team Members are expected to promptly return to work after a rest break or meal period. Team Members who demonstrate a pattern of returning to work late are subject to disciplinary action, up to and including separation.
source: PDC  NEW Meal and Rest period August 2016.docx

text: Note that rest periods are paid while meal periods are unpaid. Team Members are expected to promptly return to work after a rest break or meal period. Team Members who demonstrate a pattern of returning to work late are subject to disciplinary action, up to and including separation. Pechanga’s policy is to not combine Meal and Rest Periods, however, if business conditions warrant, it may be approved by the Division Vice President. Procedure Unpaid Meal Periods Meal periods (i.e. breakfast, lunch, dinner) are unpaid and excluded from overtime and will not be counted as hours worked if: The team member is completely relieved of all duties for the period designated as a “Meal Period”. The team member is free to leave his/her work station during the meal period; and The meal period is at least 30 minutes long. Paid Meal Periods
source: PDC  NEW Meal and Rest period August 2016.docx

text: The team member is free to leave his/her work station during the meal period; and The meal period is at least 30 minutes long. Paid Meal Periods On duty meal periods (defined as “those in which the team member is not relieved of all duties”) will be counted as hours worked and will be paid under the following circumstances and with the Supervisor or Department Manager’s prior approval: The nature of the team member’s work prevents the team member from being relieved of all duty. The team member is unable to leave his/her workstation to take a meal period because of an emergency. The team member is permitted to leave his/her work station, but must remain in the casino and be able to handle emergencies if called.
source: PDC  NEW Meal and Rest period August 2016.docx

text: The nature of the team member’s work prevents the team member from being relieved of all duty. The team member is unable to leave his/her workstation to take a meal period because of an emergency. The team member is permitted to leave his/her work station, but must remain in the casino and be able to handle emergencies if called. Team Members who demonstrate a pattern of on duty meal periods, and are able to take them, are subject to disciplinary action, up to and including separation. Missed Meal Periods Any missed meal period must be noted on the Time Clock Log each day that it occurs. It is the responsibility of both managers and team members to adhere to this procedure. Patterns of missed meal periods are subject to disciplinary action, up to and including separation.
source: PDC  NEW Meal and Rest period August 2016.docx

text: Any missed meal period must be noted on the Time Clock Log each day that it occurs. It is the responsibility of both managers and team members to adhere to this procedure. Patterns of missed meal periods are subject to disciplinary action, up to and including separation. Managers are responsible for acknowledging any missed meal periods of team members by signing the Time Clock Log on the same day the occurrences are reported. Managers are also responsible for submitting the Time Clock Logs to Payroll during each pay period. The Payroll Department will pay for missed meal period. Any trends noted by the Payroll Department should be reported to Human Resources. Any variance from this policy requires management approval.
source: PDC  NEW Meal and Rest period August 2016.docx

text: Policy and Purpose : The purpose of this policy is to standardize the process for approved holiday pay for all Non-Exempt Team Members. (Please type your change comments or explanations of the new policy below) This policy is new. Eligibility Hourly non-exempt Team Members are eligible for holiday pay for working during paid holidays. Non-eligibility Team Members who are on PTO or a leave of absence when the holiday occurs. Paid Holidays Non-exempt Team Members will receive holiday pay for working during the following paid holidays. New Year’s Eve to New Year’s Day (from 6pm New Year’s Eve to 11:59pm New Year’s Day) Memorial Day Independence Day (July 4th) Labor Day Thanksgiving Day Christmas Day Administration
source: Holiday Pay for Non-Exempt Team Members Janaury 2020.docx

text: Thanksgiving Day Christmas Day Administration Hourly non-exempt Team Members who work between the hours of 12am and 11:59pm on approved paid holidays, will receive holiday pay equal to 50 percent of their regular hourly pay rate for each hour worked in the position. Hourly non-exempt Team Members who worked in positions other than their home position between the hours of 12am and 11:59pm will receive holiday pay equal to 50 percent of the non-home position’s hourly rate for each hour worked in that position. For purposes of calculating the holiday pay, “each hour worked” means hours worked, hours spent in training, and/or hours spent in orientation. Approved paid holiday pay is not to be considered hours worked in the computation of overtime or tips.
source: Holiday Pay for Non-Exempt Team Members Janaury 2020.docx

text: Original Issue Date :	1/1/2014 SOP Title :	Report Time Pay Affected Departments :  All Non-Exempt Team Members at PR&C. Purpose: To provide partial compensation for Team Members who report to their shift and are released early because of reduced scheduling or a lack of work. Revised: Team Members will be compensated for two (2) hours at their regular rate of pay to attend a mandatory All Team Member Meeting, if they are required to attend on their regularly scheduled day off. text: SCOPE All PRC Team Members PRACTICE: Pechanga Resort and Casino has designed a program intended to provide an avenue for team members to post items of a personal nature, (personal property) intended for sale or rental. 1. Eligibility: (a) Open to all full and part time team members. 2. Guidelines: (a) Team members may post the sale or rental of personal property and/or services that does not involve an ongoing commercial enterprise or otherwise promote an outside organization or affiliation. This is to include seeking a rideshare connection. (b) The postings can be placed on the intranet (kiicha) or on any one or all four bulletin boards near wardrobe and the team member dining facilities. (c) All postings must be approved by HR/Talent Management for bulletin boards and IT for kiicha and shall be in good taste and generally of interest to other team members.

text: Policy & Purpose: A program designed to provide Team Members, Leads and Supervisors with an opportunity to receive immediate recognition for demonstrating Pechanga's Core Values and "4" Diamond Service". Recognition is acknowledged in the presentation of a ''G.E.M.'' award. Nominations for Team Members: Vice Presidents, Directors, Managers and Supervisors can nominate up to 3% of their Team Member total population each month using the criteria listed in paragraph 3. When a PR&C Team Member, Lead or Supervisor receives a ''G.E.M'' award, the form is carbonized with copies distributed as follows: The recipient keeps the white copy for their records. text: Pechanga Scholarship Program POLICY A scholarship program has been designed to benefit PR&C Team Member’s dependent children. PURPOSE Pechanga Resort & Casino is firmly committed to the value of education and continuous personal growth and learning. PR&C has developed a scholarship program, targeted specifically towards its Team Member’s children SCOPE All PR&C Team Members PRACTICE: Twelve $1,000.00 scholarships will be available on a fiscal year basis for Team Members’ dependent children who apply and are selected to pursue a degree at an accredited college or vocational school. Vice Presidents are not eligible to participate in this program. Eligibility: To be eligible to receive a scholarship, the Team Member’s child must: Be a dependent of a Regular, Full Time Member of Pechanga Resort and Casino. The Team Member’s child must plan to pursue a degree at an accredited college or vocational school.

To be eligible to receive a scholarship, the Team Member’s child must: Be a dependent of a Regular, Full Time Member of Pechanga Resort and Casino. The Team Member’s child must plan to pursue a degree at an accredited college or vocational school. Team Members may make application for scholarships for their child if they are currently enrolled. Team Members can only receive one $1,000 scholarship per household (Dependent Child). Selection Criteria: The dependent child must: provide a written essay on how this advanced learning will enhance their personal growth and development, and what they plan to do with the education when they graduate. Provide a letter of referral and/or recommendation from other than a relative describing academic or citizenship achievements. Provide proof on college/university letterhead that they are currently enrolled. Selection:

Provide a letter of referral and/or recommendation from other than a relative describing academic or citizenship achievements. Provide proof on college/university letterhead that they are currently enrolled. Selection: A committee comprising two to three members of the Pechanga Resort & Casino, led by the Team Member Programs Department will review all applications for eligibility and selection. The PDC Board will make the final decision for scholarship awards. Payment: Payment will be made to the college or university upon providing evidence of successful admission.
Original Issue Date :	April 17, 2012 SOP Title :	Tribal Preference Affected Departments : 	Applicable to all positions at PR&C

Policy and Purpose :	To identify the meaning of Tribal Preference in hiring, transfers, promotions, layoffs, and personal and/or medical leaves of absences. (Please type your change comments or explanations of the new policy below). Revision 6/19/23: The purpose of the revision is to update the format to meet PRC’s standard SOP template and add language applicable for Tribal Member-Team Members being given additional consideration for leaves of absences. Practice: PR&C will give preference in employment matters to qualified Tribal Members in the following order: Enrolled members of the Pechanga Band of Indians Tribal Member Spouses Other American Indians All other applications “Qualified” shall mean at a minimum that the Tribal Member shall meet or exceed the minimum qualifications for the position as stated on the job description. Should another candidate and a tribal member be deemed equally qualified for the position, preference will be given to the Tribal Member.

Should another candidate and a tribal member be deemed equally qualified for the position, preference will be given to the Tribal Member. Equally qualified shall mean that both candidates meet the minimum requirements and have comparable backgrounds, depth of experience, and functional experience relative to the position being filled. If more than one Tribal applicant applies for the same position, PR&C will hire the most qualified candidate, taking into consideration education, training, and work experience. Applicants entitled to Tribal preference are not selected solely on the basis of Tribal preference. All applicants must meet the minimum job qualifications to be considered for employment and preference will be applied between competing applicants. For the purpose of personal and/or medical leaves available to Team Members, Tribal Members in good standing, may be granted additional consideration in the application of these policies.

Applicants entitled to Tribal preference are not selected solely on the basis of Tribal preference. All applicants must meet the minimum job qualifications to be considered for employment and preference will be applied between competing applicants. For the purpose of personal and/or medical leaves available to Team Members, Tribal Members in good standing, may be granted additional consideration in the application of these policies. This includes, but is not limited to, bereavement, medical leaves and personal leaves of absence.
Original Issue Date :	April 15, 2015 SOP Title :	Testimony at Judicial/Administrative Proceedings Affected Departments : 	PRC ALL

Policy and Purpose :	Standard Operating Procedures for the Testimony at Judicial/Administrative Proceedings Policy. The purpose of this policy is to standardize the process of Testimony at Judicial/Administrative Proceedings Policy. It is intended to provide insight, order, and accountability for all team members subject to such policies in Testimony at Judicial/Administrative Proceedings Policy. (Please type your change comments or explanations of the new policy below). Employment Expectation During the course of your employment at Pechanga Resort & Casino (PR&C) you may be called and/or subpoenaed to testify at a judicial, administrative hearings or depositions. An example would be an unemployment insurance case. Your testimony could be necessary as a result of witnessing certain acts or events that resulted in during the course of your employment. Procedure Attendance As a PR&C Team Member you are expected to attend such hearings as part of your duties.

Procedure Attendance As a PR&C Team Member you are expected to attend such hearings as part of your duties. PR&C will work with you as it relates to your work schedule to coordinate with attendance at such hearings. Reimbursement Any time spent appearing and/or traveling to and from a hearing will be compensated at your regular rate of pay by PR&C and all out of pocket expenses, including reasonable meals, will also be compensated.

PRC Team Member Responsibility for Vendor and Visitor Access to Property Scope:  PRC All Policy & Purpose:  Standard Operating Procedure for PRC Team Member’s responsibility for all vendor/visitor access to any area on property, including back of the house, gaming floor, and all out buildings. This will include all vendor/visitors that require property access for any reason, to possess a Gaming Vendor Badge, issued by the Pechanga Gaming Commission. This will include all outside vendors and visitors that do not possess a permanent vendor badge with a picture that has been issued by the Pechanga Gaming Commission. It is the responsibility of a PRC team member to verify that all vendors are badged correctly. Procedures: Gaming Commission Check In: Vendors/Visitors needing access to any area of the property are required to check in with the Pechanga Gaming Commission prior to accessing the property.
source: PRC Team Member Responsibility for Vendor and Visitor Access to Property.docx

text: Procedures: Gaming Commission Check In: Vendors/Visitors needing access to any area of the property are required to check in with the Pechanga Gaming Commission prior to accessing the property. If the vendor/visitor does not have a PGC Vendor Picture ID Badge, a PRC team member MUST accompany the vendor/visitor at all times. It is the responsibility of the PRC team member that is accompanying the vendor to verify that the vendor is badged while on property. Responsible Department Escorting: A PRC department team member or a Management approved designee, for which the vendor/visitor is servicing, must be present and escort the vendor to all areas of the property during the vendor’s entire time on property. It is the responsibility of the PRC department team member that is accompanying the vendor to verify that the vendor is badged while on property.
source: PRC Team Member Responsibility for Vendor and Visitor Access to Property.docx

text: Responsible Department Escorting: A PRC department team member or a Management approved designee, for which the vendor/visitor is servicing, must be present and escort the vendor to all areas of the property during the vendor’s entire time on property. It is the responsibility of the PRC department team member that is accompanying the vendor to verify that the vendor is badged while on property. Vendors with issued picture badges will have access as determined by the Pechanga Gaming Commission. Violation of Policy: Any vendor/visitor/vendor that is not in compliance with this SOP, will be escorted off the property immediately. Any PRC department team member or a Management approved designee not in compliance with this SOP will be in violation of policy, and disciplinary action will be taken.
source: PRC Team Member Responsibility for Vendor and Visitor Access to Property.docx

text: Original Issue Date :	June 7, 2007 SOP Title :  	Tuition Reimbursement Program Policy Affected Departments :	PRC ALL

Policy and Purpose :	The purpose of this policy is to standardize the process of tuition reimbursement. It is intended to provide insight, order, and accountability for all Team Members, who choose to request tuition reimbursement from PRC. Updates include minor editing of titles (PRC and Organizational Development Manager) and updates in reimbursement amounts from $2,500 to $3,750 and from $5,250 to $7,500 per calendar year. Revision 9/11/23 – Minor edits in 2.d. change 4.0 to 3.0 to conform with new performance evaluation scoring standards, and in 7.b. removing Divisional VP and GM from the approval process, and making the VP of HR/Talent Management the final approver.

Policy and Purpose: PRC’s Tuition Reimbursement program is designed to reimburse up to $3,750 per calendar year (with 1 to 2 years of service), and $7,500 per calendar year (with 2+ years of service). All eligible Team Members interested to advance their education, skill level, knowledge, and career with Pechanga Resort Casino are encouraged to apply. Eligibility: To qualify for the Tuition Reimbursement program, the PRC Team Member must: Be an active full-time Team Member with twelve (12) months of continuous service. Be free of any written performance management action(s) for the previous twelve (12) months. Have demonstrated consistent and dependable attendance for the previous twelve (12) months. Have achieved an overall score of 3.0 or higher on the most recent annual performance evaluation. Program: Team Members with 1 to 2 years of service may be reimbursed up to $3,750 per calendar year for pre-approved expenses.

Have achieved an overall score of 3.0 or higher on the most recent annual performance evaluation. Program: Team Members with 1 to 2 years of service may be reimbursed up to $3,750 per calendar year for pre-approved expenses. Team Members with 2+ years of service may be reimbursed up to $7,500 per calendar year for pre-approved expenses relating to the Tuition Reimbursement program. Set limits for reimbursement apply to tuition expenses for collegiate courses as part of an Associate’s, Bachelor’s, or Master’s degree program. Requirements: All desired classes for education must be approved prior to registration by completing the tuition reimbursement program “Form A”. The course must be completed with a passing grade of “B” or higher. For courses using a different scale, approval is required from the Organizational Development Manager.

All desired classes for education must be approved prior to registration by completing the tuition reimbursement program “Form A”. The course must be completed with a passing grade of “B” or higher. For courses using a different scale, approval is required from the Organizational Development Manager. Covered Expenses: Approved covered expenses include fees for university and college credit courses, books, and associated fees related to the approved course(s): Expenses such as student fees, parking, mileage, and meals are not covered under PRC’s Tuition Reimbursement program. Certification programs are not included in PRC’s Tuition Reimbursement program. Reimbursement: Reimbursement will occur after successful completion of the course. This reimbursement qualifies for federal and state tax exemption for the PRC Team Member in the year in which it was received.

Reimbursement: Reimbursement will occur after successful completion of the course. This reimbursement qualifies for federal and state tax exemption for the PRC Team Member in the year in which it was received. If the course was not successfully completed, the PRC Team Member must seek approval from the Organizational Development Manager prior to retaking the course. Repayment of Tuition Reimbursement: Should a Team Member voluntarily/involuntarily leave his/her employment with Pechanga Resort Casino in the twelve (12) month period following reimbursement, 100% of the reimbursement shall be re-paid to Pechanga Resort Casino prior to the last day worked by the Team Member. Procedure for Tuition Reimbursement: Prior to enrolling in a course, the requesting Team Member will complete “Form A” of the Tuition Reimbursement program. The Team Member is responsible for filling out the form completely, including all of the information requested before submitting it for course approval.

Procedure for Tuition Reimbursement: Prior to enrolling in a course, the requesting Team Member will complete “Form A” of the Tuition Reimbursement program. The Team Member is responsible for filling out the form completely, including all of the information requested before submitting it for course approval. The Team Member is responsible for obtaining a signature from supervisor and/or manager, prior to submitting the forms to the Organizational Development Manager for approval. If approved, the Organizational Development Manager will forward to the VP of HR/Talent Management for final approval.. If not approved, the Organizational Development Manager will notify the Team Member of the denial. Tuition Reimbursement Tracking: Once all approval signatures are collected, the Organizational Development Manager will send the approved copy of the Tuition Reimbursement form back to the Team Member. A copy will also be kept on file in HR.

Tuition Reimbursement Tracking: Once all approval signatures are collected, the Organizational Development Manager will send the approved copy of the Tuition Reimbursement form back to the Team Member. A copy will also be kept on file in HR. After the Team Member receives the approval of “Form A” of the Tuition Reimbursement program s/he can register for the course. Upon successful completion of the course, the Team Member is required to complete “Form B” along with original documentation, and return to the Organizational Development Manager with appropriate signature from Supervisor/Manager. The Organizational Development Manager will then submit all documentation to the Accounting Department for Team Member reimbursement.
Original Issue Date :	May 14, 2013 SOP Title : Tribal Event Time Off Affected Departments : PRC ALL

Policy and Purpose :	Human Resource Standard Operating Procedures for Tribal Event Time Off. To allow Pechanga tribal team members and their spouses to be compensated for time off to attend tribal meetings, celebrations and other events without utilizing their Paid Time Off (PTO) hours. Changes made to include tribal members and spouses of tribal members currently employed at Pechanga. Procedure(s): PR&C will provide compensated time off for tribal team members and their spouses, whom are currently employed at PRC,  to attend tribal events including: Tribal meetings – Monthly PDC quarterly reviews – Quarterly Tribal funerals - Immediate and extended family Tribal ceremonies - Tribal sponsored events Tribal celebrations - Tribal sponsored events Approved tribal events as listed must be sponsored, organized or endorsed by tribal authorities. Tribal authorities include: Tribal Council, Pechanga Development Corporation, Pechanga Gaming Commission, Silver Feathers and, Cultural Committee.

Pechanga Gaming Commission, Silver Feathers and, Cultural Committee. In order to provide time off and plan for the tribal event, the team member must submit a request at least 2 weeks in advance and receive their manager’s approval. Each request shall be placed through the Self Service (MSS) system similar to other time off requests. In the event of an emergency where advance notice cannot be provided, the team member should consult directly with their manager for approval. The team member is still required to submit the request through Self Service for payroll purposes.
Original Issue Date :	10/7/2015 SOP Title :	Team Member Recognition Program Affected Departments : 	PRC_ALL

Policy and Purpose :	Standard Operating Procedures for PRC Team Member Incentive program. The purpose of this policy is to standardize the process of acknowledging and rewarding team members who consistently perform above and beyond of what is expected. It is intended to provide insight, order, and accountability for all employees at PRC. (Please type your change comments or explanations of the new policy below). Create an Incentive Program that can be used to recognize team members at PRC who consistently perform at a level exceeding expectations. Purpose: The program is intended to provide Management with an opportunity to reward and recognize Team Members who demonstrate Pechanga’s Core Values and “4” Diamond Service Guarantees. Guidelines to reward Team Members for demonstrating Pechanga’s Core Values of: a)   Pride (to include): Ownership of solving a guest challenge Enthusiasm in their work Delivering consistent quality service Exceptional dress appearance

Enthusiasm in their work Delivering consistent quality service Exceptional dress appearance Extra effort to ensure cleanliness of the property b) Passion (to include): Loyalty, dedication and commitment Fun and friendly service Adhering to Service Guarantees Team work with fellow Team Members or other departments making unforgettable first and last impressions c) Professionalism (to include): Building relationships with guests and community Honesty and having integrity being ethical in every aspect of the job knowledgeable of all specific job duties finding the answer Recognizing Team Members When Management observes a team member exceeding expectations and/or whose actions merit recognition the action should be immediately acknowledged. Recognition can be acknowledged with a token of appreciation such as a personal thank you, G.E.M. Award, inclusion in department newsletters, or public recognition in a pre-shift meeting.

Team Member/Supervisor/Manager of the Year Award Template: Human Resources SOP Non-Regulatory Version: 2 (1/25/2011) Expiration Date: Approvers: Administrators: Global Administrators Only ↓ ; Editors: Viewers: All Advanced Users - Pechanga; All Read Only Viewers - Pechanga; All Users - Pecahanga; Finance; General Manager; Global Administrators Only ↓ ; Human Resources; Internal Audit ↓ ; Commission, Gaming; Pechanga, HR; Pechanga, PDC; Non-regulatory SOPs; Non-Regulatory SOPS; PDC; PGC; Folders: Division: Property-Wide Department: VP Approval: Approved GM Approval: Approved Approval Date: 10/8/2010 SOP : Purpose: This document establishes policy, responsibilities and procedures for the annual recognition of a Team Member, Supervisor and Manager of the Year. SCOPE Applicable to all Pechanga Resort & Casino Team Members Policy: The Team Member, Supervisor, Manager of the Year program recognizes, annually, outstanding achievements for the period of 1 January through 31 December of those who have demonstrated superior job performance.
source: Team Member_Supervisor_Manager of the Year Award.pdf

text: Applicable to all Pechanga Resort & Casino Team Members Policy: The Team Member, Supervisor, Manager of the Year program recognizes, annually, outstanding achievements for the period of 1 January through 31 December of those who have demonstrated superior job performance. Nominees' achievements will reflect Pechanga's core values of ''Pride, Passion and Professionalism''. Eligibility: Any Team Member, Supervisor or Manger who meets the following criteria: Has been a Team Member, Supervisor or Manager for a minimum of one year; and Has received a rating of ''Exceeds'', or ''Outstanding'' under Pechanga's Performance Management Policy for the previous assessment period. Selection Criteria: The following criteria will be considered in the selecting the Team Member, Supervisor and Manager of the Year award: Documented consistently exceeding the expectations and criteria of all job duties. Accomplished supervisory or non-supervisory duties in an outstanding manner, setting an example of achievement for others to follow.
source: Team Member_Supervisor_Manager of the Year Award.pdf

text: Selection Criteria: The following criteria will be considered in the selecting the Team Member, Supervisor and Manager of the Year award: Documented consistently exceeding the expectations and criteria of all job duties. Accomplished supervisory or non-supervisory duties in an outstanding manner, setting an example of achievement for others to follow. Documented exemplary attendance record. Demonstrated initiative and skill in devising improved work methods and procedures or a suggestion that result in a savings in manpower, time, space, materials or other items of expense; or improved the safety and health affecting the department or entire company. Demonstrated leadership in performing duties that resulted in improved productivity of the company, including guest service, promoting teamwork and commitment to achieving excellence. Demonstrated contributions to the community or charitable volunteer organization(s). Exemplifies Pechanga Resort & Casino's Core Values of ''Pride, Passion and Professionalism''.
source: Team Member_Supervisor_Manager of the Year Award.pdf

text: Demonstrated leadership in performing duties that resulted in improved productivity of the company, including guest service, promoting teamwork and commitment to achieving excellence. Demonstrated contributions to the community or charitable volunteer organization(s). Exemplifies Pechanga Resort & Casino's Core Values of ''Pride, Passion and Professionalism''. Nomination Procedures: HR/Talent Management shall solicit nominations for the Team Member, Supervisor, and Manager of the Year award in January each year with submissions due by February 1. Each division shall submit nominations for consideration directly to the Leadership Team for approval. Each major division can submit one Team Member, Supervisor and Manager. Nominations must include the following: Nominees name, job title of current position Brief statement of current job duties Listing of any previous year awards (Team Member of the Month/Quarter, G.E.M. award, Secret Shop certificates etc.)
source: Team Member_Supervisor_Manager of the Year Award.pdf

text: Nominees name, job title of current position Brief statement of current job duties Listing of any previous year awards (Team Member of the Month/Quarter, G.E.M. award, Secret Shop certificates etc.) Narrative of the nominee's outstanding achievements based on specific facts and examples that clearly show that the achievement(s) were exceptional in accordance with the selection criteria above. Nominations shall not exceed two typed pages. Department eligibility: Team Member of the Year - the following major divisions may submit Team Member, Supervisor and Manager nominations: Financial Operations Department of Public Safety (DPS) Facilities Food & Beverage Hotel Operations Information Technology (IT) Marketing HR/Talent Management Table Games Slot Operations Selection Process: Division Directors will present their candidates to the Leadership Team for consideration. The Leadership Team will review all candidates based on the above-noted selection criteria and will select the Team Member, Supervisor, Manager of the Year.
source: Team Member_Supervisor_Manager of the Year Award.pdf

text: Facilities Food & Beverage Hotel Operations Information Technology (IT) Marketing HR/Talent Management Table Games Slot Operations Selection Process: Division Directors will present their candidates to the Leadership Team for consideration. The Leadership Team will review all candidates based on the above-noted selection criteria and will select the Team Member, Supervisor, Manager of the Year. Awards: Division nominees will be invited to a celebration with a guest that will include a formal dinner and awards. The Pechanga Team Member, Supervisor and Manager of the Year will be announced at the awards dinner. text: Original Issue Date :	 	June 14, 2021 SOP Title :  			Pechanga Dependent Scholarship Program Affected Departments :	PRC ALL

Policy and Purpose :		A scholarship program has been designed to benefit PRC Team Member’s dependent children. Pechanga Resort Casino is firmly committed to the value of education and continuous personal growth and learning. PRC has developed a scholarship program, targeted specifically towards its Team Member’s children PRACTICE: Twenty $1,000.00 scholarships will be available on a fiscal year basis for Team Members’ dependent children who apply and are selected to pursue a degree at an accredited college or vocational school. Vice Presidents are not eligible to participate in this program. Eligibility: To be eligible to receive a scholarship, the Team Member’s child must: Be a dependent of a Regular, Full Time Member of Pechanga Resort and Casino. The Team Member’s child must plan to pursue a degree at an accredited college or vocational school.

To be eligible to receive a scholarship, the Team Member’s child must: Be a dependent of a Regular, Full Time Member of Pechanga Resort and Casino. The Team Member’s child must plan to pursue a degree at an accredited college or vocational school. Team Members may make an application for a scholarship for their child if they are currently enrolled. Team Members can only receive one $1,000 scholarship per household (Dependent Child). Selection Criteria: The dependent child must: provide a written essay on how this advanced learning will enhance their personal growth and development, and what they plan to do with the education when they graduate. Provide a letter of referral and/or recommendation from other than a relative describing academic or citizenship achievements. Provide proof on college, university or vocational school letterhead that they are currently enrolled. Selection:

Provide a letter of referral and/or recommendation from other than a relative describing academic or citizenship achievements. Provide proof on college, university or vocational school letterhead that they are currently enrolled. Selection: The Team Member Programs Department will review all applications for eligibility and selection and submit to the HR/Talent Development Vice President. The HR/Talent Development Vice President will make the final decision for scholarship awards. Payment: Payment will be made to the college, university or vocational school upon approval from the HR/ Talent Development Vice President.
Original Issue Date :		July 1, 2018 Title :				Disability Accommodations Affected Departments : 		PRC_ALL

Policy and Purpose :	To provide a process for qualified disabled Team Members to request and obtain reasonable accommodations Repeals and Replaces: Fit for Duty 1 POLICY Pechanga Resort & Casino (“PRC”) does not discriminate against qualified Team Members or applicants on the basis of disability or perceived disability. Accordingly, PRC will provide a reasonable accommodation to a qualified disabled individual, as set forth below, if a reasonable accommodation would allow the individual to perform the essential functions of the job and would not impose an undue hardship on PRC. REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION PROCESS Request. The Team Member or applicant is responsible for requesting an accommodation for his or her disability. The request should be directed to the Team Member’s supervisor or Human Resources Benefits, as applicable.

Request. The Team Member or applicant is responsible for requesting an accommodation for his or her disability. The request should be directed to the Team Member’s supervisor or Human Resources Benefits, as applicable. If a supervisor is made aware of a Team Member’s need for an accommodation, the supervisor should notify Human Resources Benefits as soon as possible so that appropriate steps may be taken. Requests for an accommodation from a Team Member can be verbal or in writing and should include: Name and telephone number of the Team Member Department Supervisor A description of the accommodation the Team Member is requesting A brief explanation of how the requested accommodation will enable the Team Member to perform the essential functions of his or her job

Supervisor A description of the accommodation the Team Member is requesting A brief explanation of how the requested accommodation will enable the Team Member to perform the essential functions of his or her job A request from an applicant for employment for an accommodation should be directed to the PRC supervisor who interviews the applicant, or to Human Resources Benefits if no interview is conducted. Any PRC employee who receives a request for accommodation from an applicant should notify Human Resources Benefits as soon as possible. Discussion & Interactive Process. After receiving an oral or written request for an accommodation, PRC will reach out to the Team Member or applicant to engage in an interactive dialogue to discuss the requested accommodation and to identify and implement a reasonable accommodation if possible.

Any PRC employee who receives a request for accommodation from an applicant should notify Human Resources Benefits as soon as possible. Discussion & Interactive Process. After receiving an oral or written request for an accommodation, PRC will reach out to the Team Member or applicant to engage in an interactive dialogue to discuss the requested accommodation and to identify and implement a reasonable accommodation if possible. PRC encourages Team Members and applicants to suggest specific accommodations that would allow the Team Member or applicant to perform the essential functions of the job. However, PRC is not required to make the specific accommodation requested by the Team Member or applicant and may provide an alternative reasonable accommodation, to the extent such an accommodation can be made without imposing an undue hardship on PRC. Medical Certification.

PRC encourages Team Members and applicants to suggest specific accommodations that would allow the Team Member or applicant to perform the essential functions of the job. However, PRC is not required to make the specific accommodation requested by the Team Member or applicant and may provide an alternative reasonable accommodation, to the extent such an accommodation can be made without imposing an undue hardship on PRC. Medical Certification. PRC will determine what type of medical certification is needed, if any, to make a determination about a reasonable accommodation, given the essential functions of the job. Required medical certification may vary depending on the nature and extent of the accommodation requested. If medical certification is required, the Team Member or applicant may be asked to obtain such certification from a qualified medical professional of his or her choice and provide the documentation to Human Resources Benefits within 15 calendar days.

If PRC determines that the medical certification provided by the Team Member is insufficient, PRC may request additional information from the Team Member’s health care provider and/or require that the individual see a health care professional of PRC’s choosing, at PRC’s expense.

A reasonable accommodation will be provided if all of the following criteria are satisfied:

In each case, an individualized assessment will be conducted, the Team Member or applicant will be engaged in an interactive process, and all medical documentation provided will be reviewed. A reasonable accommodation will be provided if all of the following criteria are satisfied: The Team Member has a qualifying disability; Any and all requested medical certification has been provided to PRC by the applicable deadlines; and PRC is able to identify a reasonable accommodation that would not impose an undue hardship on PRC and would enable the Team Member or applicant to perform the essential functions of the job, now or in the foreseeable future, without endangering his or her health or safety, or the health or safety of others.
