text: USE OF LEGAL DRUGS Nothing in this Policy is intended to prohibit the lawful, customary, and appropriate purchase, use, or possession of Legal Drugs. Although Legal Drugs are not prohibited when taken in standard dosage and/or according to a physician’s prescription, any covered individual taking prescribed or over-the-counter medications will be responsible for consulting the prescribing physician and/or pharmacist to determine whether the medication may interfere with safe performance of their work. Covered individuals may not work while impaired by the use of Legal Drugs if the impairment might endanger the individual or someone else, pose a risk of significant damage to government property, or substantially interfere with their work performance. In such instances, it is the covered individual’s responsibility to use appropriate personnel procedures (e.g., call in sick, use leave, request a change of duty, or notify a supervisor) to avoid unsafe workplace practices.

PRC utilizes a competitive hiring process for all job positions. Therefore, a Team Member or applicant who reapplies for a job position pursuant to this Section I is not guaranteed employment. text: Original Issue Date :	4/12/2019 SOP Title :  	Talent Acquisition: Internal Transfers Policy Affected Departments :	All

Policy and Purpose :	The purpose of this policy is to standardize the process of internal promotions. It is intended to provide insight, order, and accountability for all Team Members subject to such policies. Transfers and promotions provide our Team Members the opportunity for career growth and advancement within Pechanga Resort Casino (PRC). Supervisors are encouraged to give considerations to internal candidates from within their own department, and those from other divisions. This policy has been revised and updated July 18, 2022, October 16, 2024, and May 15, 2025 Update May 2025 – updated occurrence and time thresholds reflecting the new PRC Attendance Policy Team Member Eligibility and Application Process A Team Member may submit an internal transfer request if: Current work status is ‘full-time,’ ‘part-time,’ or ‘on-call’. Team Members that are on PML can submit a transfer request but must meet eligibility requirements.

A Team Member may submit an internal transfer request if: Current work status is ‘full-time,’ ‘part-time,’ or ‘on-call’. Team Members that are on PML can submit a transfer request but must meet eligibility requirements. At the time of the request, the Team Member has less than nine (9) occurrences (reference: Attendance Policy), and no more than a First Notification on record. An exception may be made with the approval of the receiving Division’s Vice President, if the Team Member has a higher number of occurrences but has not had any attendance infractions in the 180 days prior to the transfer request. Current job performance meets the job expectations. The Team Member meets the job qualifications, competencies, and experience requirements for the applied position. The Team member has completed a minimum of six (6) months of successful service in their current position if applying for a position in another division.

Current job performance meets the job expectations. The Team Member meets the job qualifications, competencies, and experience requirements for the applied position. The Team member has completed a minimum of six (6) months of successful service in their current position if applying for a position in another division. Team Members who have not completed six (6) months of service are eligible for transfers and promotions within their current division, subject to the current Division’s Vice President’s approval. The Team Member informs their current Supervisor of their transfer request. Internal Transfer Approval Process Current Division Approval The Team Member’s current department supervisor must complete the internal Transfer Request by listing the Team Member’s number of occurrences in the 365-day lookback period (reference: Attendance policy), and verifying eligibility. If the Team Member is eligible, the department supervisor must approve the internal transfer request.

Current Division Approval The Team Member’s current department supervisor must complete the internal Transfer Request by listing the Team Member’s number of occurrences in the 365-day lookback period (reference: Attendance policy), and verifying eligibility. If the Team Member is eligible, the department supervisor must approve the internal transfer request. If the Team Member is not eligible, the department supervisor will reject the request and enter notes stating the reason(s). Transfer Request Review Process The Sr. Talent Acquisition Partner or designee will notify Team Member applicants of rejected requests by email. All approved requests will be reviewed to determine if age (license eligibility), eligibility, and skill requirements are met. The Talent Acquisition Partner or designee, will also review the Team Member’s file to identify any performance or attendance issues and hold further discussions with the respective HR Business Partner. Interview Process

All approved requests will be reviewed to determine if age (license eligibility), eligibility, and skill requirements are met. The Talent Acquisition Partner or designee, will also review the Team Member’s file to identify any performance or attendance issues and hold further discussions with the respective HR Business Partner. Interview Process The hiring manager will interview qualified internal applicants and make their selection. Checking of Team Member References Prior to making an employment offer, the hiring manager should contact the current manager to discuss the applicant Team Member’s performance and work background. The current manager is expected to provide an accurate reference and a copy of the latest performance evaluation and any pertinent employment-related documentation. The Sr. Talent Acquisition Partner or designee, will only extend the offer once a notification is received that all required steps have been completed. Internal Hiring Process

Internal Hiring Process When a department chooses to hire an internal Team Member for the position, the Sr. Talent Acquisition Partner or designee, will initiate the transfer Employee Action Notice (EAN), obtaining all required approvals. Gaming License (Badge) Before a Team Member may begin work in a new position, the Pechanga Gaming Commission (PGC) may require him/her to obtain a new badge that accurately reflects the job title. If the Team Member is transferring into a position that requires an upgrade, the Team Member must wait for approval of the new license/badge before starting in the new position. Seniority and Benefits

Seniority and Benefits Team Members who are transferred/promoted to fill internal job opportunities will retain their original date of hire and company benefits. Original Issue Date :		July 1, 2018 Title :				Anti-Discrimination, Anti-Harassment & Anti-Retaliation Affected Departments : 		PRC_ALL

Policy and Purpose :	To prohibit discrimination, harassment, and retaliation in employment Repeals and Replaces: Non-Harassment Non-Retaliation Policy A.	Anti-Discrimination Policy PRC prohibits discrimination against its Team Members or applicants because of race, color, religion, sex, gender, national origin, ancestry, physical or mental disability or medical condition, age for individuals over 40 years of age, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, or veteran or military status (“Protected Characteristic”). For purposes of this policy, “discrimination” means taking a tangible employment action that materially and adversely impacts the employment status, terms, conditions, privileges, opportunities, or compensation of the Team Member or applicant based on his/her actual or perceived Protected Characteristic, where the employment action would not have occurred but for the actual or perceived Protected Characteristic.

A.	Anti-Discrimination Policy PRC prohibits discrimination against its Team Members or applicants because of race, color, religion, sex, gender, national origin, ancestry, physical or mental disability or medical condition, age for individuals over 40 years of age, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, or veteran or military status (“Protected Characteristic”). For purposes of this policy, “discrimination” means taking a tangible employment action that materially and adversely impacts the employment status, terms, conditions, privileges, opportunities, or compensation of the Team Member or applicant based on his/her actual or perceived Protected Characteristic, where the employment action would not have occurred but for the actual or perceived Protected Characteristic. Nothing in this Policy is meant to preclude PRC from exercising its right under federal law to extend Native American preference in employment. B.	Anti-Harassment Policy

For purposes of this policy, “discrimination” means taking a tangible employment action that materially and adversely impacts the employment status, terms, conditions, privileges, opportunities, or compensation of the Team Member or applicant based on his/her actual or perceived Protected Characteristic, where the employment action would not have occurred but for the actual or perceived Protected Characteristic. Nothing in this Policy is meant to preclude PRC from exercising its right under federal law to extend Native American preference in employment. B.	Anti-Harassment Policy PRC prohibits harassment based on race, color, religion, sex, gender, national origin, ancestry, physical or mental disability or medical condition, age for individuals over 40 years of age, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, or veteran or military status.

Nothing in this Policy is meant to preclude PRC from exercising its right under federal law to extend Native American preference in employment. B.	Anti-Harassment Policy PRC prohibits harassment based on race, color, religion, sex, gender, national origin, ancestry, physical or mental disability or medical condition, age for individuals over 40 years of age, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, or veteran or military status. For purposes of this policy, “harassment” means unwelcome conduct in the workplace based on a Team Member’s actual or perceived Protected Characteristic that is sufficiently severe or pervasive to alter the terms and conditions of employment or unreasonably interfere with an applicant’s or Team Member’s performance, and would be considered threatening, intimidating, hostile, or abusive by a reasonable person. PRC prohibits harassment based on race, color, religion, sex, gender, national origin, ancestry, physical or mental disability or medical condition, age for individuals over 40 years of age, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, or veteran or military status. For purposes of this policy, “harassment” means unwelcome conduct in the workplace based on a Team Member’s actual or perceived Protected Characteristic that is sufficiently severe or pervasive to alter the terms and conditions of employment or unreasonably interfere with an applicant’s or Team Member’s performance, and would be considered threatening, intimidating, hostile, or abusive by a reasonable person. Prohibited harassment includes, but is not limited to, the following behavior: Verbal conduct such as epithets, derogatory jokes, slurs, comments, posts, messages, or sexual advances; Visual displays such as posters, photography, cartoons, drawings, or gestures;

Prohibited harassment includes, but is not limited to, the following behavior: Verbal conduct such as epithets, derogatory jokes, slurs, comments, posts, messages, or sexual advances; Visual displays such as posters, photography, cartoons, drawings, or gestures; Communication via electronic media of any type that includes any conduct that is prohibited by state and/or federal law or by company policy; Physical conduct including assault, unwanted touching, intentionally blocking normal movement, or other physical conduct that a reasonable person would find threatening, intimidating, or humiliating; The gratuitous sabotage or undermining of a person’s work performance; and Threats and demands to submit to sexual requests or sexual advances as a condition of continued employment, or to avoid some other loss, and offers of employment benefits in return for sexual favors.

Physical conduct including assault, unwanted touching, intentionally blocking normal movement, or other physical conduct that a reasonable person would find threatening, intimidating, or humiliating; The gratuitous sabotage or undermining of a person’s work performance; and Threats and demands to submit to sexual requests or sexual advances as a condition of continued employment, or to avoid some other loss, and offers of employment benefits in return for sexual favors. This policy prohibits harassment by or directed towards all PRC Team Members, applicants for employment, unpaid interns, volunteers, and customers; as well as persons providing services to PRC pursuant to a contract, including, but not limited to, vendors and independent contractors. All Team Members are responsible for helping to ensure the work environment is free from prohibited harassment. Team Members should also know they can be held personally liable for harassing conduct. C.	Anti-Retaliation Policy

All Team Members are responsible for helping to ensure the work environment is free from prohibited harassment. Team Members should also know they can be held personally liable for harassing conduct. C.	Anti-Retaliation Policy PRC prohibits retaliation against its Team Members and applicants. For purposes of this policy, “retaliation” means taking a tangible employment action that materially and adversely impacts the employment status, terms, conditions, privileges, opportunities, or compensation of a Team Member or applicant based on his/her Protected Activity, where the employment action would not have occurred but for the Team Member’s or applicant’s Protected Activity.

C.	Anti-Retaliation Policy PRC prohibits retaliation against its Team Members and applicants. For purposes of this policy, “retaliation” means taking a tangible employment action that materially and adversely impacts the employment status, terms, conditions, privileges, opportunities, or compensation of a Team Member or applicant based on his/her Protected Activity, where the employment action would not have occurred but for the Team Member’s or applicant’s Protected Activity. For purposes of this policy, “Protected Activity” means opposing harassing, discriminatory, or retaliatory practices; filing a claim under the Pechanga Employment Claims Act; testifying, assisting, or participating in an investigation, proceeding, or hearing conducted pursuant to the Pechanga Employment Claims Act; exercising leave rights granted by PRC policies; making a good-faith report of illegal activity or violations of PRC policies; or testifying, assisting, or participating in an investigation into illegal activity or violations of PRC policies.
